In [5]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# =========================================================
# 1. LOAD DATA
# =========================================================
ratio_file = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/quarterly_Financial_Ratios_2000_2024.csv"
crsp_file  = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/quarterly_CRSP_All_Ratios_2000_2024.csv"

# Read CSV files correctly
ratios = pd.read_csv(ratio_file, low_memory=False)
crsp   = pd.read_csv(crsp_file, low_memory=False)

# =========================================================
# 2. CLEAN COLUMN NAMES
# =========================================================
def clean_columns(df):
    df = df.copy()
    df.columns = (
        df.columns.astype(str)
        .str.strip()
        .str.replace("\ufeff", "", regex=False)
        .str.replace(r"\s+", "_", regex=True)
        .str.lower()
    )
    return df

ratios = clean_columns(ratios)
crsp   = clean_columns(crsp)

# Rename common column variants
ratios = ratios.rename(columns={
    "permno.": "permno",
    "perm_no": "permno",
    "publicdate": "public_date",
    "g_prof": "gprof"
})

crsp = crsp.rename(columns={
    "permno.": "permno",
    "perm_no": "permno"
})

print("RATIOS COLUMNS:")
print(ratios.columns.tolist())
print("\nCRSP COLUMNS:")
print(crsp.columns.tolist())

# =========================================================
# 3. CHECK REQUIRED COLUMNS
# =========================================================
required_ratio_cols = ["permno", "qdate"]
required_crsp_cols  = ["permno", "qdate"]

for col in required_ratio_cols:
    if col not in ratios.columns:
        raise ValueError(f"Missing required column in ratios file: {col}")

for col in required_crsp_cols:
    if col not in crsp.columns:
        raise ValueError(f"Missing required column in crsp file: {col}")

# =========================================================
# 4. PARSE DATES
# =========================================================
ratio_date_cols = ["qdate", "adate", "public_date"]
crsp_date_cols  = ["qdate", "date", "paydt", "rcrddt"]

for col in ratio_date_cols:
    if col in ratios.columns:
        ratios[col] = pd.to_datetime(ratios[col], errors="coerce")

for col in crsp_date_cols:
    if col in crsp.columns:
        crsp[col] = pd.to_datetime(crsp[col], errors="coerce")

# =========================================================
# 5. CONVERT NUMERIC COLUMNS
# =========================================================
ratio_numeric_cols = [
    "permno", "gvkey", "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt", "cash_debt", "debt_capital",
    "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio", "inv_turn", "at_turn", "year"
]

crsp_numeric_cols = [
    "permno", "shrcd", "siccd", "dlret", "vol", "ret", "shrout", "sprtrn", "year"
]

for col in ratio_numeric_cols:
    if col in ratios.columns:
        ratios[col] = pd.to_numeric(ratios[col], errors="coerce")

for col in crsp_numeric_cols:
    if col in crsp.columns:
        crsp[col] = pd.to_numeric(crsp[col], errors="coerce")

# Clean divyield like "6.35%"
if "divyield" in ratios.columns:
    ratios["divyield"] = (
        ratios["divyield"]
        .astype(str)
        .str.replace("%", "", regex=False)
        .str.strip()
        .replace({"": np.nan, "nan": np.nan, "none": np.nan})
    )
    ratios["divyield"] = pd.to_numeric(ratios["divyield"], errors="coerce") / 100

# =========================================================
# 6. BUILD OBSERVATION DATES
# =========================================================
# Accounting information becomes usable on public_date
if "public_date" in ratios.columns:
    ratios["obs_date"] = ratios["public_date"]
else:
    ratios["obs_date"] = pd.NaT

if "qdate" in ratios.columns:
    ratios["obs_date"] = ratios["obs_date"].fillna(ratios["qdate"])

# CRSP market date
if "date" in crsp.columns:
    crsp["mkt_date"] = crsp["date"]
else:
    crsp["mkt_date"] = pd.NaT

if "qdate" in crsp.columns:
    crsp["mkt_date"] = crsp["mkt_date"].fillna(crsp["qdate"])

# =========================================================
# 7. DROP BAD KEYS
# =========================================================
ratios = ratios.dropna(subset=["permno", "obs_date"]).copy()
crsp   = crsp.dropna(subset=["permno", "mkt_date"]).copy()

ratios["permno"] = pd.to_numeric(ratios["permno"], errors="coerce")
crsp["permno"]   = pd.to_numeric(crsp["permno"], errors="coerce")

ratios = ratios.dropna(subset=["permno"]).copy()
crsp   = crsp.dropna(subset=["permno"]).copy()

ratios["permno"] = ratios["permno"].astype(int)
crsp["permno"]   = crsp["permno"].astype(int)

# =========================================================
# 8. SORT FOR MERGE_ASOF
# VERY IMPORTANT: sort by date first, then by firm key
# =========================================================
ratios = ratios.sort_values(["obs_date", "permno"]).reset_index(drop=True)
crsp   = crsp.sort_values(["mkt_date", "permno"]).reset_index(drop=True)

# Optional checks
print("\nRatios sorted:", ratios["obs_date"].is_monotonic_increasing)
print("CRSP sorted:", crsp["mkt_date"].is_monotonic_increasing)

# =========================================================
# 9. MERGE ACCOUNTING WITH MOST RECENT AVAILABLE CRSP DATA
# =========================================================
merged = pd.merge_asof(
    ratios,
    crsp,
    left_on="obs_date",
    right_on="mkt_date",
    by="permno",
    direction="backward",
    allow_exact_matches=True,
    suffixes=("", "_crsp")
)

# =========================================================
# 10. CREATE FUTURE DISTRESS LABEL USING DLRET
# =========================================================
DLRET_THRESHOLD = -0.30
HORIZON_Q = 4

crsp_event = crsp.copy()
crsp_event = crsp_event.sort_values(["permno", "mkt_date"]).reset_index(drop=True)

# Future leads
for h in range(1, HORIZON_Q + 1):
    crsp_event[f"dlret_lead_{h}"] = crsp_event.groupby("permno")["dlret"].shift(-h)
    crsp_event[f"date_lead_{h}"] = crsp_event.groupby("permno")["mkt_date"].shift(-h)

lead_dlret_cols = [f"dlret_lead_{h}" for h in range(1, HORIZON_Q + 1)]
future_obs_cols = [f"date_lead_{h}" for h in range(1, HORIZON_Q + 1)]

# Distress from future bad delisting return
crsp_event["bad_dlret_next_4q"] = (
    crsp_event[lead_dlret_cols].le(DLRET_THRESHOLD).any(axis=1).astype(int)
)

# Exit proxy: no future observations in next 4 quarters
crsp_event["future_obs_count_4q"] = crsp_event[future_obs_cols].notna().sum(axis=1)

global_max_date = crsp_event["mkt_date"].max()
crsp_event["enough_time_to_observe"] = (
    crsp_event["mkt_date"] <= (global_max_date - pd.Timedelta(days=365))
).astype(int)

crsp_event["exit_next_4q"] = (
    (crsp_event["future_obs_count_4q"] == 0) &
    (crsp_event["enough_time_to_observe"] == 1)
).astype(int)

# Final distress label
crsp_event["distress_next_4q"] = (
    (crsp_event["bad_dlret_next_4q"] == 1) |
    (crsp_event["exit_next_4q"] == 1)
).astype(int)

# Keep only event columns for merge back
event_df = crsp_event[
    [
        "permno", "mkt_date", "bad_dlret_next_4q", "exit_next_4q",
        "distress_next_4q", "future_obs_count_4q", "enough_time_to_observe"
    ]
].copy()

# =========================================================
# 11. MERGE DISTRESS LABEL BACK TO MODEL PANEL
# =========================================================
final_df = merged.merge(event_df, on=["permno", "mkt_date"], how="left")

# =========================================================
# 12. OPTIONAL FEATURE ENGINEERING
# =========================================================
if "ret" in final_df.columns and "sprtrn" in final_df.columns:
    final_df["excess_ret"] = final_df["ret"] - final_df["sprtrn"]

for col in ["roa", "roe", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio", "npm", "gpm"]:
    if col in final_df.columns:
        final_df[f"{col}_lag1"] = final_df.groupby("permno")[col].shift(1)
        final_df[f"{col}_chg1"] = final_df[col] - final_df[f"{col}_lag1"]

# =========================================================
# 13. REMOVE CENSORED OBSERVATIONS FOR MODELING
# =========================================================
model_df = final_df.copy()
if "enough_time_to_observe" in model_df.columns:
    model_df = model_df[model_df["enough_time_to_observe"] == 1].copy()

# =========================================================
# 14. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

final_df.to_csv(output_dir + "merged_credit_panel_with_distress_label.csv", index=False)
model_df.to_csv(output_dir + "model_ready_credit_panel.csv", index=False)

print("Files saved successfully.")
print(output_dir + "merged_credit_panel_with_distress_label.csv")
print(output_dir + "model_ready_credit_panel.csv")

RATIOS COLUMNS:
['permno', 'qdate', 'gvkey', 'adate', 'public_date', 'bm', 'npm', 'gpm', 'roa', 'roe', 'gprof', 'invt_act', 'rect_act', 'curr_debt', 'cash_debt', 'debt_capital', 'de_ratio', 'quick_ratio', 'curr_ratio', 'inv_turn', 'at_turn', 'divyield', 'ticker', 'cusip', 'year']

CRSP COLUMNS:
['permno', 'qdate', 'date', 'shrcd', 'siccd', 'ncusip', 'ticker', 'comnam', 'shrcls', 'cusip', 'paydt', 'rcrddt', 'dlret', 'vol', 'ret', 'shrout', 'sprtrn', 'year']

Ratios sorted: True
CRSP sorted: True
Files saved successfully.
/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/merged_credit_panel_with_distress_label.csv
/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/model_ready_credit_panel.csv
In [6]:
# =========================================================
# 17. REGIME / CRISIS PERIOD ANALYSIS
# Paste this RIGHT AFTER your yearly distress charts section
# Uses model_df because that is the active dataset here
# =========================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. COPY DATA
# ---------------------------------------------------------
regime_df = model_df.copy()

# Make sure date/year exists
regime_df["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(regime_df["obs_date"])
regime_df["obs_year"] = regime_df["obs_date"].dt.year

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. DEFINE REGIMES
# ---------------------------------------------------------
def assign_regime(year):
    if 2000 <= year <= 2002:
        return "Dot-com / Early Stress"
    elif 2003 <= year <= 2007:
        return "Pre-GFC Expansion"
    elif 2008 <= year <= 2009:
        return "Global Financial Crisis"
    elif 2010 <= year <= 2014:
        return "Post-GFC Recovery"
    elif 2015 <= year <= 2019:
        return "Late-cycle Normalization"
    elif 2020 <= year <= 2021:
        return "COVID Shock"
    elif 2022 <= year <= 2024:
        return "Post-COVID Tightening"
    else:
        return "Other"

regime_order = [
    "Dot-com / Early Stress",
    "Pre-GFC Expansion",
    "Global Financial Crisis",
    "Post-GFC Recovery",
    "Late-cycle Normalization",
    "COVID Shock",
    "Post-COVID Tightening",
    "Other"
]

regime_df["regime"] = regime_df["obs_year"].apply(assign_regime)
regime_df["regime"] = pd.Categorical(regime_df["regime"], categories=regime_order, ordered=True)

# Simple binary stress flag too
regime_df["stress_flag"] = np.where(
    regime_df["regime"].isin([
        "Dot-com / Early Stress",
        "Global Financial Crisis",
        "COVID Shock",
        "Post-COVID Tightening"
    ]),
    "Stress Period",
    "Non-Stress Period"
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. REGIME-LEVEL SUMMARY
# ---------------------------------------------------------
regime_summary = (
    regime_df.groupby("regime", observed=False)
    .agg(
        obs_count=("distress_next_4q", "count"),
        distress_count=("distress_next_4q", "sum"),
        distress_rate=("distress_next_4q", "mean"),
        bad_dlret_count=("bad_dlret_next_4q", "sum"),
        exit_count=("exit_next_4q", "sum"),
        bad_dlret_rate=("bad_dlret_next_4q", "mean"),
        exit_rate=("exit_next_4q", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

# contribution shares inside distress
regime_summary["bad_dlret_share_of_distress"] = (
    regime_summary["bad_dlret_count"] / regime_summary["distress_count"]
)
regime_summary["exit_share_of_distress"] = (
    regime_summary["exit_count"] / regime_summary["distress_count"]
)

print("\nREGIME SUMMARY")
display(
    regime_summary.style
    .background_gradient(
        subset=[
            "distress_rate", "bad_dlret_rate", "exit_rate",
            "bad_dlret_share_of_distress", "exit_share_of_distress"
        ],
        cmap="RdYlGn_r"
    )
    .background_gradient(
        subset=["obs_count", "distress_count", "bad_dlret_count", "exit_count"],
        cmap="Blues"
    )
    .format({
        "obs_count": "{:,.0f}",
        "distress_count": "{:,.0f}",
        "bad_dlret_count": "{:,.0f}",
        "exit_count": "{:,.0f}",
        "distress_rate": "{:.2%}",
        "bad_dlret_rate": "{:.2%}",
        "exit_rate": "{:.2%}",
        "bad_dlret_share_of_distress": "{:.2%}",
        "exit_share_of_distress": "{:.2%}"
    })
    .set_caption("Regime-level distress summary")
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. STRESS VS NON-STRESS SUMMARY
# ---------------------------------------------------------
stress_summary = (
    regime_df.groupby("stress_flag")
    .agg(
        obs_count=("distress_next_4q", "count"),
        distress_count=("distress_next_4q", "sum"),
        distress_rate=("distress_next_4q", "mean"),
        bad_dlret_rate=("bad_dlret_next_4q", "mean"),
        exit_rate=("exit_next_4q", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

print("\nSTRESS VS NON-STRESS SUMMARY")
display(
    stress_summary.style
    .background_gradient(
        subset=["distress_rate", "bad_dlret_rate", "exit_rate"],
        cmap="RdYlGn_r"
    )
    .background_gradient(
        subset=["obs_count", "distress_count"],
        cmap="Blues"
    )
    .format({
        "obs_count": "{:,.0f}",
        "distress_count": "{:,.0f}",
        "distress_rate": "{:.2%}",
        "bad_dlret_rate": "{:.2%}",
        "exit_rate": "{:.2%}"
    })
    .set_caption("Stress vs non-stress periods")
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. BEST PRESENTATION CHART:
# REGIME-LEVEL DISTRESS RATE
# ---------------------------------------------------------
plot_df = regime_summary[regime_summary["regime"] != "Other"].copy()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
bars = plt.bar(plot_df["regime"], plot_df["distress_rate"])

for i, v in enumerate(plot_df["distress_rate"]):
    plt.text(i, v + 0.001, f"{v:.1%}", ha="center", fontsize=10)

plt.title("Distress Rate by Economic Regime", fontsize=14, weight="bold")
plt.xlabel("Regime")
plt.ylabel("Distress Rate")
plt.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)
plt.xticks(rotation=25, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. VERY GOOD PRESENTATION CHART:
# STACKED COMPOSITION OF DISTRESS
# ---------------------------------------------------------
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.bar(plot_df["regime"], plot_df["bad_dlret_rate"], label="Bad DLRET rate")
plt.bar(
    plot_df["regime"],
    plot_df["exit_rate"],
    bottom=plot_df["bad_dlret_rate"],
    label="Exit rate"
)

plt.title("Distress Composition by Regime", fontsize=14, weight="bold")
plt.xlabel("Regime")
plt.ylabel("Rate")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)
plt.xticks(rotation=25, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7. CONNECTED DOT CHART:
# TOTAL DISTRESS VS COMPONENTS
# ---------------------------------------------------------
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(plot_df["regime"], plot_df["distress_rate"], marker="o", linewidth=2.5, label="Total distress rate")
plt.plot(plot_df["regime"], plot_df["bad_dlret_rate"], marker="o", linewidth=2.5, label="Bad DLRET rate")
plt.plot(plot_df["regime"], plot_df["exit_rate"], marker="o", linewidth=2.5, label="Exit rate")

plt.title("Distress and Its Components Across Regimes", fontsize=14, weight="bold")
plt.xlabel("Regime")
plt.ylabel("Rate")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.xticks(rotation=25, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 8. SAVE TABLES
# ---------------------------------------------------------
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"
regime_summary.to_csv(f"{output_dir}/regime_distress_summary.csv", index=False)
stress_summary.to_csv(f"{output_dir}/stress_vs_nonstress_summary.csv", index=False)

print("Saved:")
print(f"{output_dir}/regime_distress_summary.csv")
print(f"{output_dir}/stress_vs_nonstress_summary.csv")

REGIME SUMMARY

In [4]:
# =========================================================
# 16. DATA AUDIT / CROSS-CHECK
# =========================================================
import os
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

print("=" * 80)
print("DATA AUDIT FOR CREDIT MODEL READINESS")
print("=" * 80)

# -------------------------------
# A. BASIC SHAPE AND COVERAGE
# -------------------------------
print("\n[1] BASIC SHAPE")
print("-" * 80)
print("ratios shape:", ratios.shape)
print("crsp shape:", crsp.shape)
print("final_df shape:", final_df.shape)
print("model_df shape:", model_df.shape)

print("\nUnique firms")
print("ratios permno:", ratios["permno"].nunique())
print("crsp permno:", crsp["permno"].nunique())
print("final_df permno:", final_df["permno"].nunique())
print("model_df permno:", model_df["permno"].nunique())

if "obs_date" in final_df.columns:
    print("\nobs_date range:", final_df["obs_date"].min(), "to", final_df["obs_date"].max())
if "mkt_date" in final_df.columns:
    print("mkt_date range:", final_df["mkt_date"].min(), "to", final_df["mkt_date"].max())

# -------------------------------
# B. DUPLICATES
# -------------------------------
print("\n[2] DUPLICATE CHECK")
print("-" * 80)
dup_final = final_df.duplicated(subset=["permno", "obs_date"]).sum() if "obs_date" in final_df.columns else np.nan
dup_model = model_df.duplicated(subset=["permno", "obs_date"]).sum() if "obs_date" in model_df.columns else np.nan

print("Duplicate permno-obs_date rows in final_df:", dup_final)
print("Duplicate permno-obs_date rows in model_df:", dup_model)

# -------------------------------
# C. MERGE QUALITY
# -------------------------------
print("\n[3] MERGE QUALITY")
print("-" * 80)

merge_cols = [c for c in ["mkt_date", "ret", "vol", "shrout", "siccd", "sprtrn"] if c in final_df.columns]
merge_quality = pd.DataFrame({
    "variable": merge_cols,
    "non_missing_pct": [final_df[c].notna().mean() for c in merge_cols],
    "missing_pct": [final_df[c].isna().mean() for c in merge_cols]
})
print(merge_quality)

if "obs_date" in final_df.columns and "mkt_date" in final_df.columns:
    final_df["merge_gap_days"] = (final_df["obs_date"] - final_df["mkt_date"]).dt.days
    print("\nGap between obs_date and matched mkt_date:")
    print(final_df["merge_gap_days"].describe(percentiles=[0.01, 0.05, 0.5, 0.95, 0.99]))

# -------------------------------
# D. TARGET CHECK
# -------------------------------
print("\n[4] DISTRESS LABEL CHECK")
print("-" * 80)

for c in ["bad_dlret_next_4q", "exit_next_4q", "distress_next_4q", "enough_time_to_observe"]:
    if c in model_df.columns:
        print(f"\n{c}:")
        print(model_df[c].value_counts(dropna=False))

if "distress_next_4q" in model_df.columns:
    distress_rate = model_df["distress_next_4q"].mean()
    print("\nOverall distress rate:", distress_rate)

if "obs_date" in model_df.columns and "distress_next_4q" in model_df.columns:
    model_df["obs_year"] = model_df["obs_date"].dt.year
    yearly_distress = (
        model_df.groupby("obs_year")["distress_next_4q"]
        .agg(obs_count="count", distress_rate="mean", distress_count="sum")
        .reset_index()
    )
    print("\nYearly distress summary:")
    print(yearly_distress.tail(20))

# -------------------------------
# E. MISSINGNESS IN KEY VARIABLES
# -------------------------------
print("\n[5] MISSINGNESS IN CORE VARIABLES")
print("-" * 80)

core_vars = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt", "cash_debt",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "inv_turn", "at_turn", "divyield",
    "ret", "vol", "shrout", "sprtrn", "siccd", "excess_ret"
]

available_core_vars = [c for c in core_vars if c in model_df.columns]

missing_report = pd.DataFrame({
    "variable": available_core_vars,
    "missing_pct": [model_df[c].isna().mean() for c in available_core_vars],
    "non_missing_pct": [model_df[c].notna().mean() for c in available_core_vars]
}).sort_values("missing_pct", ascending=False)

print(missing_report)

# -------------------------------
# F. DISTRIBUTION CHECK
# -------------------------------
print("\n[6] DISTRIBUTION CHECK")
print("-" * 80)

dist_vars = ["roa", "roe", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio", "ret", "excess_ret"]
for c in dist_vars:
    if c in model_df.columns:
        print(f"\nSummary stats for {c}")
        print(model_df[c].describe(percentiles=[0.01, 0.05, 0.50, 0.95, 0.99]))

# -------------------------------
# G. ECONOMIC SANITY CHECK
# -------------------------------
print("\n[7] ECONOMIC SANITY CHECK")
print("-" * 80)

compare_vars = ["roa", "roe", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio", "ret", "excess_ret"]
compare_vars = [c for c in compare_vars if c in model_df.columns]

if "distress_next_4q" in model_df.columns and len(compare_vars) > 0:
    means_by_target = model_df.groupby("distress_next_4q")[compare_vars].mean()
    print(means_by_target)

# -------------------------------
# H. READINESS CHECKLIST
# -------------------------------
print("\n[8] FINAL READINESS CHECK")
print("-" * 80)

checklist = {
    "No duplicate firm-date rows": (dup_model == 0),
    "Target exists": ("distress_next_4q" in model_df.columns),
    "Target has both 0 and 1": (model_df["distress_next_4q"].nunique() == 2) if "distress_next_4q" in model_df.columns else False,
    "Enough firms (>100)": (model_df["permno"].nunique() > 100),
    "Enough rows (>1000)": (len(model_df) > 1000),
    "Market data mostly merged (>80% mkt_date non-missing)": (model_df["mkt_date"].notna().mean() > 0.80) if "mkt_date" in model_df.columns else False,
    "At least 10 core variables with >70% coverage": ((missing_report["non_missing_pct"] > 0.70).sum() >= 10) if len(missing_report) > 0 else False
}

for k, v in checklist.items():
    print(f"{k}: {v}")

all_good = all(checklist.values())

print("\nMODEL READINESS DECISION")
print("-" * 80)
if all_good:
    print("READY: dataset looks good for baseline modeling.")
else:
    print("NOT FULLY READY: fix weak areas before modeling.")

# -------------------------------
# I. SAVE AUDIT OUTPUTS
# -------------------------------
print("\n[9] SAVING AUDIT FILES")
print("-" * 80)

output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

missing_report.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_missingness_report.csv"), index=False)

if "obs_date" in model_df.columns and "distress_next_4q" in model_df.columns:
    yearly_distress.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_yearly_distress_summary.csv"), index=False)

if "distress_next_4q" in model_df.columns and len(compare_vars) > 0:
    means_by_target.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_predictor_means_by_distress.csv"))

merge_quality.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_merge_quality.csv"), index=False)

checklist_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "check": list(checklist.keys()),
    "passed": list(checklist.values())
})
checklist_df.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_readiness_checklist.csv"), index=False)

print("Saved:")
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_missingness_report.csv"))
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_yearly_distress_summary.csv"))
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_predictor_means_by_distress.csv"))
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_merge_quality.csv"))
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "audit_readiness_checklist.csv"))

In [12]:
# =========================================================
# 18. PRE-DISTRESS TRAJECTORY ANALYSIS
# What happens 1,2,3,4,5,6 quarters before distress?
# =========================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. COPY DATA
# ---------------------------------------------------------
traj_df = model_df.copy()

traj_df["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(traj_df["obs_date"])
traj_df = traj_df.sort_values(["permno", "obs_date"]).reset_index(drop=True)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. DEFINE VARIABLES TO STUDY
# ---------------------------------------------------------
study_vars = [
    "roa", "roe", "gpm", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio",
    "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn",
    "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

study_vars = [c for c in study_vars if c in traj_df.columns]
print("Variables used:", study_vars)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. CREATE AN EVENT QUARTER
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Since distress_next_4q = 1 means distress happens within next 4 quarters,
# we approximate the event quarter as the FIRST quarter where the forward label turns on,
# shifted ahead by 4 quarters.
#
# This gives a practical event-time anchor for pre-distress path analysis.

traj_df["row_num"] = traj_df.groupby("permno").cumcount()

# first row where distress_next_4q == 1 for each firm
first_signal = (
    traj_df.loc[traj_df["distress_next_4q"] == 1]
    .groupby("permno", as_index=False)
    .agg(first_signal_row=("row_num", "min"))
)

traj_df = traj_df.merge(first_signal, on="permno", how="left")

# approximate event row = first signal row + 4 quarters
traj_df["event_row_approx"] = traj_df["first_signal_row"] + 4

# actual event date approximation within each firm
event_dates = (
    traj_df.loc[:, ["permno", "row_num", "obs_date"]]
    .rename(columns={"row_num": "event_row_approx", "obs_date": "event_date_approx"})
)

traj_df = traj_df.merge(event_dates, on=["permno", "event_row_approx"], how="left")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. DISTANCE TO EVENT IN QUARTERS
# ---------------------------------------------------------
traj_df["quarters_to_event"] = traj_df["event_row_approx"] - traj_df["row_num"]

# We want 1..6 quarters BEFORE event
pre_distress_df = traj_df[
    traj_df["quarters_to_event"].isin([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6])
].copy()

print("Pre-distress rows:", pre_distress_df.shape)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. SUMMARY TABLE: MEAN TRAJECTORY BY QUARTERS TO EVENT
# ---------------------------------------------------------
trajectory_summary = (
    pre_distress_df
    .groupby("quarters_to_event")[study_vars]
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("quarters_to_event", ascending=False)
)

print("\nPRE-DISTRESS TRAJECTORY SUMMARY")
display(
    trajectory_summary.style
    .background_gradient(cmap="RdYlGn_r", axis=None)
    .format("{:.4f}", subset=study_vars)
    .set_caption("Average variable values 1 to 6 quarters before distress")
)

Variables used: ['roa', 'roe', 'gpm', 'gprof', 'debt_capital', 'de_ratio', 'quick_ratio', 'curr_ratio', 'at_turn', 'ret', 'excess_ret', 'vol', 'shrout']
Pre-distress rows: (15694, 69)

PRE-DISTRESS TRAJECTORY SUMMARY
Average variable values 1 to 6 quarters before distress

1 2.000000 -0.2624 -1.5208 -4.3873 0.1133 0.0818 3.9795 1.7579 2.1320 0.8918 -0.0955 -0.0823 242275.5318 45048.8369
0 1.000000 -0.2981 -1.6946 -5.0570 0.0644 0.6893 -1.9264 1.5231 1.8640 0.9238 -0.1384 -0.1251 289578.6565 45665.1917
In [11]:
# =========================================================
# 20. COMBINED PRE-DISTRESS INDEX CHART
# Standardize variables so they are comparable on one chart
# =========================================================

index_vars = [
    "roa", "roe", "npm", "gprof",       # fundamentals
    "de_ratio", "debt_capital",         # leverage
    "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",        # liquidity
    "ret", "excess_ret", "vol"          # market
]
index_vars = [c for c in index_vars if c in trajectory_summary.columns]

ts = trajectory_summary.copy()

# z-score across the 6-quarter path for each variable
for col in index_vars:
    std = ts[col].std()
    if pd.notna(std) and std > 0:
        ts[col + "_z"] = (ts[col] - ts[col].mean()) / std
    else:
        ts[col + "_z"] = np.nan

group_map = {
    "fundamental": [c for c in ["roa", "roe", "gprof"] if c in ts.columns],
    "leverage": [c for c in ["de_ratio", "debt_capital"] if c in ts.columns],
    "liquidity": [c for c in ["quick_ratio", "curr_ratio"] if c in ts.columns],
    "market": [c for c in ["ret", "excess_ret", "vol"] if c in ts.columns]
}

group_series = []
for group_name, cols in group_map.items():
    zcols = [c + "_z" for c in cols if c + "_z" in ts.columns]
    if len(zcols) > 0:
        ts[group_name + "_index"] = ts[zcols].mean(axis=1)
        group_series.append(group_name + "_index")

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5.5))
for col in group_series:
    plt.plot(
        ts["quarters_to_event"],
        ts[col],
        marker="o",
        linewidth=2.5,
        label=col.replace("_index", "").title()
    )

plt.gca().invert_xaxis()
plt.axvline(1, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
plt.title("Average Pre-Distress Path: Fundamentals vs Leverage vs Liquidity vs Market", fontsize=13, weight="bold")
plt.xlabel("Quarters before distress")
plt.ylabel("Standardized trajectory")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [13]:
# =========================================================
# 21. TEST FOR LATE-STAGE CRASH
# Compare early pre-distress period (6 to 3 quarters before)
# vs late pre-distress period (2 to 1 quarters before)
# =========================================================

pre_distress_df["phase"] = np.where(
    pre_distress_df["quarters_to_event"].isin([1, 2]),
    "Late stage (t-2 to t-1)",
    np.where(
        pre_distress_df["quarters_to_event"].isin([3, 4, 5, 6]),
        "Earlier stage (t-6 to t-3)",
        np.nan
    )
)

phase_compare = (
    pre_distress_df.dropna(subset=["phase"])
    .groupby("phase")[study_vars]
    .mean()
    .T
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"index": "variable"})
)

if "Earlier stage (t-6 to t-3)" in phase_compare.columns and "Late stage (t-2 to t-1)" in phase_compare.columns:
    phase_compare["late_minus_early"] = (
        phase_compare["Late stage (t-2 to t-1)"] -
        phase_compare["Earlier stage (t-6 to t-3)"]
    )

print("\nEARLY VS LATE PRE-DISTRESS COMPARISON")
display(
    phase_compare.style
    .background_gradient(subset=["late_minus_early"], cmap="RdYlGn_r")
    .format("{:.4f}", subset=[c for c in phase_compare.columns if c != "variable"])
    .set_caption("Do variables worsen sharply near the final 1–2 quarters?")
)

EARLY VS LATE PRE-DISTRESS COMPARISON
Do variables worsen sharply near the final 1–2 quarters?

In [5]:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Make sure obs_year exists
model_df["obs_year"] = model_df["obs_date"].dt.year

# Yearly summary
yearly_summary = (
    model_df.groupby("obs_year")
    .agg(
        obs_count=("distress_next_4q", "count"),
        distress_count=("distress_next_4q", "sum"),
        distress_rate=("distress_next_4q", "mean"),
        bad_dlret_count=("bad_dlret_next_4q", "sum"),
        exit_count=("exit_next_4q", "sum")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

print(yearly_summary)

# ---------------------------
# 1. YEARLY DISTRESS RATE
# ---------------------------
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.plot(yearly_summary["obs_year"], yearly_summary["distress_rate"], marker="o")
plt.axvspan(2008, 2009, alpha=0.2)
plt.axvspan(2020, 2020, alpha=0.2)
plt.title("Yearly Distress Rate")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Distress Rate")
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

# ---------------------------
# 2. YEARLY DISTRESS COUNT
# ---------------------------
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.bar(yearly_summary["obs_year"], yearly_summary["distress_count"])
plt.title("Yearly Distress Count")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Number of Distressed Observations")
plt.grid(True, axis="y")
plt.show()

# ---------------------------
# 3. DECOMPOSITION OF DISTRESS EVENT
# ---------------------------
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.plot(yearly_summary["obs_year"], yearly_summary["bad_dlret_count"], marker="o", label="Bad DLRET")
plt.plot(yearly_summary["obs_year"], yearly_summary["exit_count"], marker="o", label="Exit")
plt.title("Components of Distress Event by Year")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Count")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

In [6]:
yearly_components = (
    model_df.groupby(model_df["obs_date"].dt.year)
    .agg(
        distress_rate=("distress_next_4q", "mean"),
        distress_count=("distress_next_4q", "sum"),
        bad_dlret_rate=("bad_dlret_next_4q", "mean"),
        exit_rate=("exit_next_4q", "mean"),
        bad_dlret_count=("bad_dlret_next_4q", "sum"),
        exit_count=("exit_next_4q", "sum")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

print(yearly_components[yearly_components["obs_date"].between(2000, 2004)])

   obs_date  distress_rate  distress_count  bad_dlret_rate  exit_rate  \
0      2000       0.051951             739        0.033322   0.018629   
1      2001       0.050292            1238        0.032377   0.017915   
2      2002       0.044816             995        0.029367   0.015449   
3      2003       0.027583             565        0.014304   0.013279   
4      2004       0.015400             301        0.008289   0.007112

In [7]:
subset = yearly_components[yearly_components["obs_date"].between(2000, 2004)]

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
plt.plot(subset["obs_date"], subset["distress_rate"], marker="o", label="Total distress rate")
plt.plot(subset["obs_date"], subset["bad_dlret_rate"], marker="o", label="Bad DLRET rate")
plt.plot(subset["obs_date"], subset["exit_rate"], marker="o", label="Exit rate")
plt.title("Distress Components, 2000–2004")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Rate")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
In [8]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import os

# =========================================================
# 1. COPY MODEL DATA
# =========================================================
clean_df = model_df.copy()

# =========================================================
# 2. CHOOSE VARIABLES TO WINSORIZE
# =========================================================
winsor_vars = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt", "cash_debt",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "inv_turn", "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

winsor_vars = [c for c in winsor_vars if c in clean_df.columns]

# =========================================================
# 3. SAVE ORIGINAL VALUES FOR COMPARISON
# =========================================================
original_df = clean_df[winsor_vars].copy()

# =========================================================
# 4. WINSORIZATION FUNCTION
# =========================================================
def winsorize_series(s, lower=0.01, upper=0.99):
    if s.notna().sum() == 0:
        return s
    lo = s.quantile(lower)
    hi = s.quantile(upper)
    return s.clip(lower=lo, upper=hi)

# =========================================================
# 5. APPLY WINSORIZATION
# =========================================================
for col in winsor_vars:
    clean_df[col] = winsorize_series(clean_df[col], 0.01, 0.99)

# =========================================================
# 6. CREATE MISSING FLAGS FOR IMPORTANT PARTIALLY-MISSING VARIABLES
# =========================================================
flag_vars = [
    "roe", "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt",
    "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio", "inv_turn", "divyield"
]

flag_vars = [c for c in flag_vars if c in clean_df.columns]

for col in flag_vars:
    clean_df[f"{col}_missing"] = clean_df[col].isna().astype(int)

# =========================================================
# 7. OPTIONAL: DROP VERY HIGH-MISSING VARIABLES FOR FIRST MODEL
# =========================================================
# divyield had high missingness in your audit, so we exclude it from model v1
if "divyield" in clean_df.columns:
    clean_df = clean_df.drop(columns=["divyield"])
In [9]:
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]
In [10]:
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, brier_score_loss
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# =========================================================
# 1. FEATURE SET
# =========================================================
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

features = [c for c in features if c in clean_df.columns]
target = "distress_next_4q"

# =========================================================
# 2. CREATE YEAR
# =========================================================
clean_df["obs_year"] = clean_df["obs_date"].dt.year

# =========================================================
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# =========================================================
train_df = clean_df[(clean_df["obs_year"] >= 2000) & (clean_df["obs_year"] <= 2016)].copy()
valid_df = clean_df[(clean_df["obs_year"] >= 2017) & (clean_df["obs_year"] <= 2020)].copy()
test_df  = clean_df[(clean_df["obs_year"] >= 2021) & (clean_df["obs_year"] <= 2024)].copy()

X_train = train_df[features]
y_train = train_df[target]

X_valid = valid_df[features]
y_valid = valid_df[target]

X_test = test_df[features]
y_test = test_df[target]

print("Train shape:", X_train.shape)
print("Validation shape:", X_valid.shape)
print("Test shape:", X_test.shape)

print("Train distress rate:", y_train.mean())
print("Validation distress rate:", y_valid.mean())
print("Test distress rate:", y_test.mean())

# =========================================================
# 4. LOGISTIC MODEL PIPELINE
# =========================================================
logit_model = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(max_iter=2000, class_weight="balanced"))
])

logit_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# =========================================================
# 5. PREDICT PROBABILITIES
# =========================================================
p_train = logit_model.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
p_valid = logit_model.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
p_test  = logit_model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 6. EVALUATION FUNCTION
# =========================================================
def evaluate_split(y_true, p, name):
    auc = roc_auc_score(y_true, p)
    pr_auc = average_precision_score(y_true, p)
    brier = brier_score_loss(y_true, p)

    print(f"\n{name}")
    print(f"ROC-AUC: {auc:.4f}")
    print(f"PR-AUC:  {pr_auc:.4f}")
    print(f"Brier:   {brier:.4f}")

evaluate_split(y_train, p_train, "TRAIN")
evaluate_split(y_valid, p_valid, "VALIDATION")
evaluate_split(y_test, p_test, "TEST")

# =========================================================
# 7. TEST-SET RISK DECILES
# =========================================================
test_results = test_df[["permno", "obs_date", target]].copy()
test_results["pred_pd"] = p_test
test_results["risk_decile"] = pd.qcut(test_results["pred_pd"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1

decile_summary = (
    test_results.groupby("risk_decile")
    .agg(
        obs_count=(target, "count"),
        realized_distress_rate=(target, "mean"),
        avg_pred_pd=("pred_pd", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("risk_decile")
)

print("\nTEST DECILE SUMMARY")
print(decile_summary)

# =========================================================
# 8. COEFFICIENT TABLE
# =========================================================
coef = logit_model.named_steps["model"].coef_[0]
coef

Train shape: (297771, 14)
Validation shape: (56871, 14)
Test shape: (50768, 14)
Train distress rate: 0.02642634776388567
Validation distress rate: 0.015016440716709746
Test distress rate: 0.020642924676961865

ROC-AUC: 0.8065
PR-AUC:  0.1287
Brier:   0.1708

VALIDATION
ROC-AUC: 0.7852
PR-AUC:  0.0571
Brier:   0.1945

TEST
ROC-AUC: 0.8126
PR-AUC:  0.1095
Brier:   0.2086

TEST DECILE SUMMARY
   risk_decile  obs_count  realized_distress_rate  avg_pred_pd
0            1       5077                0.004136     0.098272
1            2       5077                0.002955     0.183447
2            3       5077                0.003742     0.235797
3            4       5076                0.006895     0.282862
4            5       5077                0.007091     0.334071

6            7       5076                0.010047     0.458522

9           10       5077                0.101635     0.885799

        0.46350137, -0.03240873,  0.65945072, -0.77573458,  0.19961257,
       -0.36559908,  0.09339656,  0.19178198, -0.48340538])
In [11]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import (
    roc_auc_score,
    average_precision_score,
    brier_score_loss
)
from sklearn.calibration import calibration_curve

# =========================================================
# 1. FEATURE SET
# =========================================================
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

features = [c for c in features if c in clean_df.columns]
target = "distress_next_4q"

# =========================================================
# 2. CREATE YEAR
# =========================================================
clean_df["obs_year"] = clean_df["obs_date"].dt.year

# =========================================================
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# =========================================================
train_df = clean_df[(clean_df["obs_year"] >= 2000) & (clean_df["obs_year"] <= 2016)].copy()
valid_df = clean_df[(clean_df["obs_year"] >= 2017) & (clean_df["obs_year"] <= 2020)].copy()
test_df  = clean_df[(clean_df["obs_year"] >= 2021) & (clean_df["obs_year"] <= 2024)].copy()

X_train = train_df[features]
y_train = train_df[target]

X_valid = valid_df[features]
y_valid = valid_df[target]

X_test = test_df[features]
y_test = test_df[target]

print("Train shape:", X_train.shape)
print("Validation shape:", X_valid.shape)
print("Test shape:", X_test.shape)

print("Train distress rate:", y_train.mean())
print("Validation distress rate:", y_valid.mean())
print("Test distress rate:", y_test.mean())

# =========================================================
# 4. MODEL DEFINITIONS
# =========================================================
logit_plain = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(max_iter=2000))
])

logit_balanced = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(max_iter=2000, class_weight="balanced"))
])

# =========================================================
# 5. FIT MODELS
# =========================================================
logit_plain.fit(X_train, y_train)
logit_balanced.fit(X_train, y_train)

# =========================================================
# 6. PREDICT PROBABILITIES
# =========================================================
preds = {}

for name, model in {
    "plain": logit_plain,
    "balanced": logit_balanced
}.items():
    preds[name] = {
        "train": model.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1],
        "valid": model.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1],
        "test": model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1],
    }

# =========================================================
# 7. METRICS FUNCTION
# =========================================================
def evaluate_split(y_true, p, model_name, split_name):
    auc = roc_auc_score(y_true, p)
    pr_auc = average_precision_score(y_true, p)
    brier = brier_score_loss(y_true, p)
    avg_pred = np.mean(p)
    avg_real = np.mean(y_true)

    return {
        "model": model_name,
        "split": split_name,
        "roc_auc": auc,
        "pr_auc": pr_auc,
        "brier": brier,
        "avg_pred_pd": avg_pred,
        "realized_rate": avg_real
    }

metric_rows = []

for model_name in preds:
    metric_rows.append(evaluate_split(y_train, preds[model_name]["train"], model_name, "train"))
    metric_rows.append(evaluate_split(y_valid, preds[model_name]["valid"], model_name, "validation"))
    metric_rows.append(evaluate_split(y_test, preds[model_name]["test"], model_name, "test"))

metrics_df = pd.DataFrame(metric_rows)
print("\nMETRICS TABLE")
print(metrics_df)

# =========================================================
# 8. DECILE CALIBRATION TABLE
# =========================================================
def build_decile_table(df, y_true, p, model_name, split_name):
    out = df[["permno", "obs_date"]].copy()
    out["y_true"] = y_true.values if hasattr(y_true, "values") else y_true
    out["pred_pd"] = p
    out["decile"] = pd.qcut(out["pred_pd"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1

    decile_table = (
        out.groupby("decile")
        .agg(
            obs_count=("y_true", "count"),
            realized_distress_rate=("y_true", "mean"),
            avg_pred_pd=("pred_pd", "mean"),
            distress_count=("y_true", "sum")
        )
        .reset_index()
        .sort_values("decile")
    )

    decile_table["model"] = model_name
    decile_table["split"] = split_name
    decile_table["calibration_gap"] = decile_table["avg_pred_pd"] - decile_table["realized_distress_rate"]

    return decile_table

decile_tables = []

for model_name in preds:
    decile_tables.append(build_decile_table(train_df, y_train, preds[model_name]["train"], model_name, "train"))
    decile_tables.append(build_decile_table(valid_df, y_valid, preds[model_name]["valid"], model_name, "validation"))
    decile_tables.append(build_decile_table(test_df, y_test, preds[model_name]["test"], model_name, "test"))

deciles_df = pd.concat(decile_tables, ignore_index=True)

print("\nTEST DECILE TABLE - PLAIN LOGIT")
print(deciles_df[(deciles_df["model"] == "plain") & (deciles_df["split"] == "test")])

print("\nTEST DECILE TABLE - BALANCED LOGIT")
print(deciles_df[(deciles_df["model"] == "balanced") & (deciles_df["split"] == "test")])

# =========================================================
# 9. CALIBRATION CURVES
# =========================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))

for model_name, label in [("plain", "Plain Logit"), ("balanced", "Balanced Logit")]:
    frac_pos, mean_pred = calibration_curve(y_test, preds[model_name]["test"], n_bins=10, strategy="quantile")
    plt.plot(mean_pred, frac_pos, marker="o", label=label)

plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")
plt.title("Calibration Curve - Test Set")
plt.xlabel("Mean Predicted Probability")
plt.ylabel("Observed Distress Rate")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 10. PREDICTED VS REALIZED BY DECILE (TEST)
# =========================================================
for model_name, chart_title in [("plain", "Plain Logit"), ("balanced", "Balanced Logit")]:
    test_dec = deciles_df[(deciles_df["model"] == model_name) & (deciles_df["split"] == "test")].copy()

    plt.figure(figsize=(9, 5))
    plt.plot(test_dec["decile"], test_dec["avg_pred_pd"], marker="o", label="Average Predicted PD")
    plt.plot(test_dec["decile"], test_dec["realized_distress_rate"], marker="o", label="Realized Distress Rate")
    plt.title(f"Predicted vs Realized Distress by Decile - {chart_title}")
    plt.xlabel("Risk Decile")
    plt.ylabel("Rate")
    plt.legend()
    plt.grid(True)
    plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 11. COEFFICIENT TABLES
# =========================================================
def get_coef_table(model, features, model_name):
    coef = model.named_steps["model"].coef_[0]
    out = pd.DataFrame({
        "variable": features,
        "coefficient": coef,
        "abs_coefficient": np.abs(coef),
        "model": model_name
    }).sort_values("abs_coefficient", ascending=False)
    return out

coef_plain = get_coef_table(logit_plain, features, "plain")
coef_bal = get_coef_table(logit_balanced, features, "balanced")

print("\nCOEFFICIENT TABLE - PLAIN LOGIT")
print(coef_plain)

print("\nCOEFFICIENT TABLE - BALANCED LOGIT")
print(coef_bal)

# =========================================================
# 12. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

metrics_df.to_csv(output_dir + "logit_calibration_metrics.csv", index=False)
deciles_df.to_csv(output_dir + "logit_calibration_deciles.csv", index=False)
coef_plain.to_csv(output_dir + "logit_plain_coefficients.csv", index=False)
coef_bal.to_csv(output_dir + "logit_balanced_coefficients.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved:")
print(output_dir + "logit_calibration_metrics.csv")
print(output_dir + "logit_calibration_deciles.csv")
print(output_dir + "logit_plain_coefficients.csv")
print(output_dir + "logit_balanced_coefficients.csv")

Train shape: (297771, 14)
Validation shape: (56871, 14)
Test shape: (50768, 14)
Train distress rate: 0.02642634776388567
Validation distress rate: 0.015016440716709746
Test distress rate: 0.020642924676961865

In [12]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, brier_score_loss, roc_curve

# =========================================================
# 1. LOAD THE MODEL-READY DATASET YOU ALREADY CREATED
# =========================================================
data_file = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/model_ready_credit_panel.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(data_file, low_memory=False)

# parse dates
df["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["obs_date"], errors="coerce")
if "mkt_date" in df.columns:
    df["mkt_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["mkt_date"], errors="coerce")

df["obs_year"] = df["obs_date"].dt.year
TARGET = "distress_next_4q"

print("Loaded shape:", df.shape)
print("Columns:", df.columns.tolist()[:20], "...")
print("Target mean:", df[TARGET].mean())

# =========================================================
# 2. CLEAN TARGET
# =========================================================
df = df[df["obs_date"].notna()].copy()
df = df[df[TARGET].notna()].copy()
df[TARGET] = pd.to_numeric(df[TARGET], errors="coerce")
df = df[df[TARGET].notna()].copy()
df[TARGET] = df[TARGET].astype(int)

# =========================================================
# 3. FEATURE SET
# =========================================================
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt", "cash_debt",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "inv_turn", "at_turn", "divyield",
    "ret", "vol", "sprtrn", "excess_ret"
]

features = [c for c in features if c in df.columns]

# add size proxy
if "shrout" in df.columns:
    df["log_shrout"] = np.log1p(pd.to_numeric(df["shrout"], errors="coerce").clip(lower=0))
    features.append("log_shrout")

features = list(dict.fromkeys(features))
print("Using features:", features)

# =========================================================
# 4. TIME SPLIT
# =========================================================
train_df = df[df["obs_year"].between(2000, 2016)].copy()
valid_df = df[df["obs_year"].between(2017, 2020)].copy()
test_df  = df[df["obs_year"].between(2021, 2024)].copy()

print("\nSplit sizes")
print("Train:", train_df.shape, "rate =", train_df[TARGET].mean())
print("Valid:", valid_df.shape, "rate =", valid_df[TARGET].mean())
print("Test :", test_df.shape,  "rate =", test_df[TARGET].mean())

# =========================================================
# 5. WINSORIZE USING TRAIN ONLY
# =========================================================
def get_winsor_bounds(frame, cols, lower=0.01, upper=0.99):
    bounds = {}
    for c in cols:
        s = pd.to_numeric(frame[c], errors="coerce").dropna()
        if len(s) > 0:
            bounds[c] = (s.quantile(lower), s.quantile(upper))
    return bounds

def apply_winsor_bounds(frame, bounds):
    out = frame.copy()
    for c, (lo, hi) in bounds.items():
        out[c] = pd.to_numeric(out[c], errors="coerce").clip(lower=lo, upper=hi)
    return out

winsor_bounds = get_winsor_bounds(train_df, features, lower=0.01, upper=0.99)

train_df = apply_winsor_bounds(train_df, winsor_bounds)
valid_df = apply_winsor_bounds(valid_df, winsor_bounds)
test_df  = apply_winsor_bounds(test_df, winsor_bounds)

# =========================================================
# 6. BUILD MATRICES
# =========================================================
X_train = train_df[features].copy()
y_train = train_df[TARGET].copy()

X_valid = valid_df[features].copy()
y_valid = valid_df[TARGET].copy()

X_test = test_df[features].copy()
y_test = test_df[TARGET].copy()

# =========================================================
# 7. RIDGE PIPELINE
# =========================================================
preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ("num", Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler())
        ]), features)
    ],
    remainder="drop"
)

ridge_grid = [0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 3.0, 10.0, 30.0, 100.0]

ridge_results = []
ridge_models = {}

for C in ridge_grid:
    model = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocessor),
        ("clf", LogisticRegression(
            penalty="l2",
            C=C,
            solver="lbfgs",
            max_iter=3000,
            class_weight=None
        ))
    ])

    model.fit(X_train, y_train)

    p_train = model.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
    p_valid = model.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
    p_test  = model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

    ridge_results.append({
        "C": C,
        "train_auc": roc_auc_score(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_auc": roc_auc_score(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_auc": roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test),
        "train_brier": brier_score_loss(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_brier": brier_score_loss(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_brier": brier_score_loss(y_test, p_test),
        "train_avg_pred": p_train.mean(),
        "valid_avg_pred": p_valid.mean(),
        "test_avg_pred": p_test.mean(),
        "train_realized": y_train.mean(),
        "valid_realized": y_valid.mean(),
        "test_realized": y_test.mean()
    })

    ridge_models[C] = model

ridge_results = pd.DataFrame(ridge_results).sort_values(
    ["valid_auc", "valid_brier"],
    ascending=[False, True]
)

print("\nRIDGE TUNING RESULTS")
print(ridge_results)

best_C = ridge_results.iloc[0]["C"]
best_ridge = ridge_models[best_C]
print("\nBest C:", best_C)

# =========================================================
# 8. FINAL PERFORMANCE
# =========================================================
def evaluate_split(split_name, y, p):
    return {
        "split": split_name,
        "auc": roc_auc_score(y, p),
        "brier": brier_score_loss(y, p),
        "avg_pred_prob": p.mean(),
        "realized_distress_rate": y.mean()
    }

p_train = best_ridge.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
p_valid = best_ridge.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
p_test  = best_ridge.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

ridge_eval = pd.DataFrame([
    evaluate_split("train", y_train, p_train),
    evaluate_split("valid", y_valid, p_valid),
    evaluate_split("test", y_test, p_test)
])

print("\nBEST RIDGE PERFORMANCE")
print(ridge_eval)

# =========================================================
# 9. TEST DECILE REPORT
# =========================================================
test_out = pd.DataFrame({
    "y_true": y_test.values,
    "p_hat": p_test
})

test_out["decile"] = pd.qcut(test_out["p_hat"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1

decile_report = (
    test_out.groupby("decile")
    .agg(
        obs=("y_true", "count"),
        distress_rate=("y_true", "mean"),
        avg_pred_prob=("p_hat", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("decile")
)

print("\nRIDGE TEST DECILE REPORT")
print(decile_report)

# =========================================================
# 10. ROC CURVE
# =========================================================
fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_test, p_test)
auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"Ridge Logit (AUC={auc:.4f})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("Ridge Logistic ROC Curve - Test Sample")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 11. COEFFICIENTS
# =========================================================
clf = best_ridge.named_steps["clf"]

coef_table = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": features,
    "coef": clf.coef_.ravel()
}).sort_values("coef", ascending=False)

print("\nTop positive ridge coefficients")
print(coef_table.head(10))

print("\nTop negative ridge coefficients")
print(coef_table.tail(10))

# =========================================================
# 12. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

ridge_results.to_csv(output_dir + "ridge_tuning_results.csv", index=False)
ridge_eval.to_csv(output_dir + "ridge_eval_summary.csv", index=False)
decile_report.to_csv(output_dir + "ridge_test_decile_report.csv", index=False)
coef_table.to_csv(output_dir + "ridge_coefficients.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved files:")
print(output_dir + "ridge_tuning_results.csv")
print(output_dir + "ridge_eval_summary.csv")
print(output_dir + "ridge_test_decile_report.csv")
print(output_dir + "ridge_coefficients.csv")

In [13]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import KBinsDiscretizer
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, brier_score_loss, roc_curve

# =========================================================
# 1. LOAD MODEL-READY DATA
# =========================================================
data_file = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/model_ready_credit_panel.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(data_file, low_memory=False)

df["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["obs_date"], errors="coerce")
df["obs_year"] = df["obs_date"].dt.year

TARGET = "distress_next_4q"

df = df[df["obs_date"].notna()].copy()
df = df[df[TARGET].notna()].copy()
df[TARGET] = pd.to_numeric(df[TARGET], errors="coerce")
df = df[df[TARGET].notna()].copy()
df[TARGET] = df[TARGET].astype(int)

# =========================================================
# 2. CLEANER FEATURE SET FOR SCORECARD
# remove overlapping variables to keep interpretation cleaner
# =========================================================
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio",
    "quick_ratio",
    "inv_turn", "at_turn",
    "divyield",
    "vol", "excess_ret"
]

features = [c for c in features if c in df.columns]

if "shrout" in df.columns:
    df["log_shrout"] = np.log1p(pd.to_numeric(df["shrout"], errors="coerce").clip(lower=0))
    features.append("log_shrout")

features = list(dict.fromkeys(features))
print("Using scorecard features:", features)

# =========================================================
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# =========================================================
train_df = df[df["obs_year"].between(2000, 2016)].copy()
valid_df = df[df["obs_year"].between(2017, 2020)].copy()
test_df  = df[df["obs_year"].between(2021, 2024)].copy()

print("\nSplit sizes")
print("Train:", train_df.shape, "rate =", train_df[TARGET].mean())
print("Valid:", valid_df.shape, "rate =", valid_df[TARGET].mean())
print("Test :", test_df.shape,  "rate =", test_df[TARGET].mean())

# =========================================================
# 4. WINSORIZE USING TRAIN ONLY
# =========================================================
def get_winsor_bounds(frame, cols, lower=0.01, upper=0.99):
    bounds = {}
    for c in cols:
        s = pd.to_numeric(frame[c], errors="coerce").dropna()
        if len(s) > 0:
            bounds[c] = (s.quantile(lower), s.quantile(upper))
    return bounds

def apply_winsor_bounds(frame, bounds):
    out = frame.copy()
    for c, (lo, hi) in bounds.items():
        out[c] = pd.to_numeric(out[c], errors="coerce").clip(lower=lo, upper=hi)
    return out

winsor_bounds = get_winsor_bounds(train_df, features, lower=0.01, upper=0.99)

train_df = apply_winsor_bounds(train_df, winsor_bounds)
valid_df = apply_winsor_bounds(valid_df, winsor_bounds)
test_df  = apply_winsor_bounds(test_df, winsor_bounds)

X_train = train_df[features].copy()
y_train = train_df[TARGET].copy()

X_valid = valid_df[features].copy()
y_valid = valid_df[TARGET].copy()

X_test = test_df[features].copy()
y_test = test_df[TARGET].copy()

# =========================================================
# 5. SCORECARD-STYLE BINNED LOGIT
# =========================================================
# quantile bins = easier interpretation and more stable than equal-width
preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ("num_bins", Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("bins", KBinsDiscretizer(
                n_bins=5,
                encode="onehot-dense",
                strategy="quantile"
            ))
        ]), features)
    ],
    remainder="drop"
)

scorecard_grid = [0.1, 1.0, 3.0, 10.0]
scorecard_results = []
scorecard_models = {}

for C in scorecard_grid:
    model = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocessor),
        ("clf", LogisticRegression(
            penalty="l2",
            C=C,
            solver="lbfgs",
            max_iter=4000,
            class_weight=None
        ))
    ])

    model.fit(X_train, y_train)

    p_train = model.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
    p_valid = model.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
    p_test  = model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

    scorecard_results.append({
        "C": C,
        "train_auc": roc_auc_score(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_auc": roc_auc_score(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_auc": roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test),
        "train_brier": brier_score_loss(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_brier": brier_score_loss(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_brier": brier_score_loss(y_test, p_test),
        "train_avg_pred": p_train.mean(),
        "valid_avg_pred": p_valid.mean(),
        "test_avg_pred": p_test.mean()
    })

    scorecard_models[C] = model

scorecard_results = pd.DataFrame(scorecard_results).sort_values(
    ["valid_auc", "valid_brier"],
    ascending=[False, True]
)

print("\nSCORECARD TUNING RESULTS")
print(scorecard_results)

best_C = scorecard_results.iloc[0]["C"]
best_scorecard = scorecard_models[best_C]
print("\nBest scorecard C:", best_C)

# =========================================================
# 6. FINAL PERFORMANCE
# =========================================================
def evaluate_split(split_name, y, p):
    return {
        "split": split_name,
        "auc": roc_auc_score(y, p),
        "brier": brier_score_loss(y, p),
        "avg_pred_prob": p.mean(),
        "realized_distress_rate": y.mean()
    }

p_train = best_scorecard.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
p_valid = best_scorecard.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
p_test  = best_scorecard.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

scorecard_eval = pd.DataFrame([
    evaluate_split("train", y_train, p_train),
    evaluate_split("valid", y_valid, p_valid),
    evaluate_split("test", y_test, p_test)
])

print("\nBEST SCORECARD PERFORMANCE")
print(scorecard_eval)

# =========================================================
# 7. TEST DECILE REPORT
# =========================================================
test_out = pd.DataFrame({
    "y_true": y_test.values,
    "p_hat": p_test
})

test_out["decile"] = pd.qcut(test_out["p_hat"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1

decile_report = (
    test_out.groupby("decile")
    .agg(
        obs=("y_true", "count"),
        distress_rate=("y_true", "mean"),
        avg_pred_prob=("p_hat", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("decile")
)

print("\nSCORECARD TEST DECILE REPORT")
print(decile_report)

# =========================================================
# 8. ROC CURVE
# =========================================================
fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_test, p_test)
auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"Scorecard Logit (AUC={auc:.4f})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("Scorecard ROC Curve - Test Sample")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 9. SCORECARD POINTS TABLE
# =========================================================
prep = best_scorecard.named_steps["prep"]
clf = best_scorecard.named_steps["clf"]

bin_names = prep.get_feature_names_out()
coefs = clf.coef_.ravel()

points_table = pd.DataFrame({
    "bin_feature": bin_names,
    "coef": coefs
}).sort_values("coef", ascending=False)

# simple points conversion
PDO = 20
factor = PDO / np.log(2)
points_table["points"] = np.round(points_table["coef"] * factor, 0).astype(int)

print("\nTop score-increasing bins")
print(points_table.head(20))

print("\nTop score-reducing bins")
print(points_table.tail(20))

# =========================================================
# 10. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

scorecard_results.to_csv(output_dir + "scorecard_tuning_results.csv", index=False)
scorecard_eval.to_csv(output_dir + "scorecard_eval_summary.csv", index=False)
decile_report.to_csv(output_dir + "scorecard_test_decile_report.csv", index=False)
points_table.to_csv(output_dir + "scorecard_points_table.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved files:")
print(output_dir + "scorecard_tuning_results.csv")
print(output_dir + "scorecard_eval_summary.csv")
print(output_dir + "scorecard_test_decile_report.csv")
print(output_dir + "scorecard_points_table.csv")

Using scorecard features: ['bm', 'npm', 'gpm', 'roa', 'roe', 'gprof', 'debt_capital', 'de_ratio', 'quick_ratio', 'inv_turn', 'at_turn', 'divyield', 'vol', 'excess_ret', 'log_shrout']

Split sizes
Train: (297771, 66) rate = 0.02642634776388567
Valid: (56871, 66) rate = 0.015016440716709746
Test : (50768, 66) rate = 0.020642924676961865

/opt/anaconda3/envs/life123/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sklearn/preprocessing/_discretization.py:397: UserWarning: Bins whose width are too small (i.e., <= 1e-8) in feature 11 are removed. Consider decreasing the number of bins.
  warnings.warn(
/opt/anaconda3/envs/life123/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sklearn/preprocessing/_discretization.py:296: FutureWarning: The current default behavior, quantile_method='linear', will be changed to quantile_method='averaged_inverted_cdf' in scikit-learn version 1.9 to naturally support sample weight equivalence properties by default. Pass quantile_method='averaged_inverted_cdf' explicitly to silence this warning.
  warnings.warn(

  warnings.warn(
