In [14]:
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio",
    "quick_ratio",
    "inv_turn", "at_turn",
    "divyield",
    "vol", "excess_ret", "log_shrout"
]
In [15]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, brier_score_loss, roc_curve
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

# =========================================================
# 1. LOAD MODEL-READY DATA
# =========================================================
data_file = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/model_ready_credit_panel.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(data_file, low_memory=False)

df["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["obs_date"], errors="coerce")
if "mkt_date" in df.columns:
    df["mkt_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["mkt_date"], errors="coerce")

df["obs_year"] = df["obs_date"].dt.year
TARGET = "distress_next_4q"

# keep usable rows only
df = df[df["obs_date"].notna()].copy()
df = df[df[TARGET].notna()].copy()
df[TARGET] = pd.to_numeric(df[TARGET], errors="coerce")
df = df[df[TARGET].notna()].copy()
df[TARGET] = df[TARGET].astype(int)

print("Loaded shape:", df.shape)
print("Target rate:", df[TARGET].mean())

# =========================================================
# 2. FEATURE SET
# keep this comparable to scorecard / cleaner than first ridge
# =========================================================
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio",
    "quick_ratio",
    "inv_turn", "at_turn",
    "divyield",
    "vol", "excess_ret"
]

features = [c for c in features if c in df.columns]

if "shrout" in df.columns:
    df["log_shrout"] = np.log1p(pd.to_numeric(df["shrout"], errors="coerce").clip(lower=0))
    features.append("log_shrout")

features = list(dict.fromkeys(features))
print("Using features:", features)

# =========================================================
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# =========================================================
train_df = df[df["obs_year"].between(2000, 2016)].copy()
valid_df = df[df["obs_year"].between(2017, 2020)].copy()
test_df  = df[df["obs_year"].between(2021, 2024)].copy()

print("\nSplit sizes")
print("Train:", train_df.shape, "rate =", train_df[TARGET].mean())
print("Valid:", valid_df.shape, "rate =", valid_df[TARGET].mean())
print("Test :", test_df.shape,  "rate =", test_df[TARGET].mean())

# =========================================================
# 4. WINSORIZE USING TRAIN ONLY
# RF does not need scaling, but winsorization still helps
# =========================================================
def get_winsor_bounds(frame, cols, lower=0.01, upper=0.99):
    bounds = {}
    for c in cols:
        s = pd.to_numeric(frame[c], errors="coerce").dropna()
        if len(s) > 0:
            bounds[c] = (s.quantile(lower), s.quantile(upper))
    return bounds

def apply_winsor_bounds(frame, bounds):
    out = frame.copy()
    for c, (lo, hi) in bounds.items():
        out[c] = pd.to_numeric(out[c], errors="coerce").clip(lower=lo, upper=hi)
    return out

winsor_bounds = get_winsor_bounds(train_df, features, lower=0.01, upper=0.99)

train_df = apply_winsor_bounds(train_df, winsor_bounds)
valid_df = apply_winsor_bounds(valid_df, winsor_bounds)
test_df  = apply_winsor_bounds(test_df, winsor_bounds)

# =========================================================
# 5. BUILD MATRICES
# =========================================================
X_train = train_df[features].copy()
y_train = train_df[TARGET].copy()

X_valid = valid_df[features].copy()
y_valid = valid_df[TARGET].copy()

X_test = test_df[features].copy()
y_test = test_df[TARGET].copy()

# impute missing values
imputer = SimpleImputer(strategy="median")
X_train_imp = pd.DataFrame(imputer.fit_transform(X_train), columns=features, index=X_train.index)
X_valid_imp = pd.DataFrame(imputer.transform(X_valid), columns=features, index=X_valid.index)
X_test_imp  = pd.DataFrame(imputer.transform(X_test), columns=features, index=X_test.index)

# =========================================================
# 6. RANDOM FOREST TUNING
# =========================================================
rf_grid = [
    {"n_estimators": 300, "max_depth": 6,  "min_samples_leaf": 100, "max_features": "sqrt"},
    {"n_estimators": 300, "max_depth": 8,  "min_samples_leaf": 50,  "max_features": "sqrt"},
    {"n_estimators": 400, "max_depth": 10, "min_samples_leaf": 25,  "max_features": "sqrt"},
    {"n_estimators": 400, "max_depth": 12, "min_samples_leaf": 25,  "max_features": 0.5},
    {"n_estimators": 500, "max_depth": None, "min_samples_leaf": 10, "max_features": "sqrt"}
]

rf_results = []
rf_models = {}

for i, params in enumerate(rf_grid, start=1):
    model = RandomForestClassifier(
        n_estimators=params["n_estimators"],
        max_depth=params["max_depth"],
        min_samples_leaf=params["min_samples_leaf"],
        max_features=params["max_features"],
        class_weight="balanced_subsample",
        random_state=42,
        n_jobs=-1
    )

    model.fit(X_train_imp, y_train)

    p_train = model.predict_proba(X_train_imp)[:, 1]
    p_valid = model.predict_proba(X_valid_imp)[:, 1]
    p_test  = model.predict_proba(X_test_imp)[:, 1]

    rf_results.append({
        "model_id": i,
        "n_estimators": params["n_estimators"],
        "max_depth": params["max_depth"],
        "min_samples_leaf": params["min_samples_leaf"],
        "max_features": params["max_features"],
        "train_auc": roc_auc_score(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_auc": roc_auc_score(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_auc": roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test),
        "train_brier": brier_score_loss(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_brier": brier_score_loss(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_brier": brier_score_loss(y_test, p_test),
        "train_avg_pred": p_train.mean(),
        "valid_avg_pred": p_valid.mean(),
        "test_avg_pred": p_test.mean(),
        "train_realized": y_train.mean(),
        "valid_realized": y_valid.mean(),
        "test_realized": y_test.mean()
    })

    rf_models[i] = model

rf_results = pd.DataFrame(rf_results).sort_values(
    ["valid_auc", "valid_brier"],
    ascending=[False, True]
)

print("\nRANDOM FOREST TUNING RESULTS")
print(rf_results)

best_model_id = int(rf_results.iloc[0]["model_id"])
best_rf = rf_models[best_model_id]

print("\nBest RF model_id:", best_model_id)
print(rf_results.iloc[0])

# =========================================================
# 7. FINAL PERFORMANCE
# =========================================================
def evaluate_split(split_name, y, p):
    return {
        "split": split_name,
        "auc": roc_auc_score(y, p),
        "brier": brier_score_loss(y, p),
        "avg_pred_prob": p.mean(),
        "realized_distress_rate": y.mean()
    }

p_train = best_rf.predict_proba(X_train_imp)[:, 1]
p_valid = best_rf.predict_proba(X_valid_imp)[:, 1]
p_test  = best_rf.predict_proba(X_test_imp)[:, 1]

rf_eval = pd.DataFrame([
    evaluate_split("train", y_train, p_train),
    evaluate_split("valid", y_valid, p_valid),
    evaluate_split("test", y_test, p_test)
])

print("\nBEST RANDOM FOREST PERFORMANCE")
print(rf_eval)

# =========================================================
# 8. TEST DECILE REPORT
# =========================================================
test_out = pd.DataFrame({
    "y_true": y_test.values,
    "p_hat": p_test
})

test_out["decile"] = pd.qcut(test_out["p_hat"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1

rf_decile_report = (
    test_out.groupby("decile")
    .agg(
        obs=("y_true", "count"),
        distress_rate=("y_true", "mean"),
        avg_pred_prob=("p_hat", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("decile")
)

print("\nRANDOM FOREST TEST DECILE REPORT")
print(rf_decile_report)

# =========================================================
# 9. CALIBRATION TABLE
# =========================================================
rf_calibration = rf_decile_report.copy()
print("\nRANDOM FOREST CALIBRATION TABLE")
print(rf_calibration)

# =========================================================
# 10. TOP-DECILE CAPTURE
# =========================================================
def top_bucket_capture(y_true, p_hat, top_share=0.10):
    temp = pd.DataFrame({
        "y_true": np.asarray(y_true),
        "p_hat": np.asarray(p_hat)
    }).copy()

    cutoff = temp["p_hat"].quantile(1 - top_share)
    temp["top_bucket"] = (temp["p_hat"] >= cutoff).astype(int)

    total_events = temp["y_true"].sum()
    captured_events = temp.loc[temp["top_bucket"] == 1, "y_true"].sum()
    capture_rate = captured_events / total_events if total_events > 0 else np.nan

    return pd.DataFrame([{
        "top_share": top_share,
        "total_events": int(total_events),
        "captured_events": int(captured_events),
        "capture_rate": capture_rate
    }])

rf_top_decile_capture = top_bucket_capture(y_test, p_test, top_share=0.10)
print("\nRANDOM FOREST TOP-DECILE CAPTURE")
print(rf_top_decile_capture)

# =========================================================
# 11. ROC CURVE
# =========================================================
fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_test, p_test)
auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"Random Forest (AUC={auc:.4f})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("Random Forest ROC Curve - Test Sample")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 12. DECILE FIGURE
# =========================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.plot(rf_decile_report["decile"], rf_decile_report["distress_rate"], marker="o", label="Realized Distress Rate")
plt.plot(rf_decile_report["decile"], rf_decile_report["avg_pred_prob"], marker="o", label="Average Predicted Probability")
plt.xlabel("Risk Decile (1 = safest, 10 = riskiest)")
plt.ylabel("Rate / Probability")
plt.title("Random Forest Test Deciles")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 13. FEATURE IMPORTANCE
# =========================================================
rf_importance = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": features,
    "importance": best_rf.feature_importances_
}).sort_values("importance", ascending=False)

print("\nRANDOM FOREST FEATURE IMPORTANCE")
print(rf_importance)

top_imp = rf_importance.head(12).sort_values("importance", ascending=True)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.barh(top_imp["feature"], top_imp["importance"])
plt.xlabel("Importance")
plt.ylabel("Feature")
plt.title("Top Random Forest Feature Importances")
plt.grid(True, axis="x")
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 14. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

rf_results.to_csv(output_dir + "rf_tuning_results.csv", index=False)
rf_eval.to_csv(output_dir + "rf_eval_summary.csv", index=False)
rf_decile_report.to_csv(output_dir + "rf_test_decile_report.csv", index=False)
rf_calibration.to_csv(output_dir + "rf_test_calibration.csv", index=False)
rf_top_decile_capture.to_csv(output_dir + "rf_top_decile_capture.csv", index=False)
rf_importance.to_csv(output_dir + "rf_feature_importance.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved files:")
print(output_dir + "rf_tuning_results.csv")
print(output_dir + "rf_eval_summary.csv")
print(output_dir + "rf_test_decile_report.csv")
print(output_dir + "rf_test_calibration.csv")
print(output_dir + "rf_top_decile_capture.csv")
print(output_dir + "rf_feature_importance.csv")

In [16]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.ensemble import HistGradientBoostingClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, brier_score_loss, roc_curve

# =========================================================
# 1. USE EXISTING SPLITS
# Assumes these already exist:
# X_train, y_train, X_valid, y_valid, X_test, y_test
# =========================================================

# Impute first because HistGradientBoosting does not like missing values in some workflows
imputer = SimpleImputer(strategy="median")

X_train_imp = pd.DataFrame(imputer.fit_transform(X_train), columns=X_train.columns, index=X_train.index)
X_valid_imp = pd.DataFrame(imputer.transform(X_valid), columns=X_valid.columns, index=X_valid.index)
X_test_imp  = pd.DataFrame(imputer.transform(X_test),  columns=X_test.columns,  index=X_test.index)

# =========================================================
# 2. GRID
# =========================================================
hgb_grid = [
    {"max_depth": 3, "learning_rate": 0.03, "max_iter": 200, "min_samples_leaf": 100},
    {"max_depth": 3, "learning_rate": 0.05, "max_iter": 300, "min_samples_leaf": 100},
    {"max_depth": 4, "learning_rate": 0.03, "max_iter": 300, "min_samples_leaf": 75},
    {"max_depth": 4, "learning_rate": 0.05, "max_iter": 400, "min_samples_leaf": 50},
    {"max_depth": 5, "learning_rate": 0.03, "max_iter": 400, "min_samples_leaf": 50},
]

hgb_results = []
hgb_models = {}

# =========================================================
# 3. TRAIN
# =========================================================
for i, params in enumerate(hgb_grid, start=1):
    model = HistGradientBoostingClassifier(
        loss="log_loss",
        max_depth=params["max_depth"],
        learning_rate=params["learning_rate"],
        max_iter=params["max_iter"],
        min_samples_leaf=params["min_samples_leaf"],
        random_state=42
    )

    model.fit(X_train_imp, y_train)

    p_train = model.predict_proba(X_train_imp)[:, 1]
    p_valid = model.predict_proba(X_valid_imp)[:, 1]
    p_test  = model.predict_proba(X_test_imp)[:, 1]

    hgb_results.append({
        "model_id": i,
        "max_depth": params["max_depth"],
        "learning_rate": params["learning_rate"],
        "max_iter": params["max_iter"],
        "min_samples_leaf": params["min_samples_leaf"],
        "train_auc": roc_auc_score(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_auc": roc_auc_score(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_auc": roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test),
        "train_pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_test, p_test),
        "train_brier": brier_score_loss(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_brier": brier_score_loss(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_brier": brier_score_loss(y_test, p_test),
        "train_avg_pred": p_train.mean(),
        "valid_avg_pred": p_valid.mean(),
        "test_avg_pred": p_test.mean(),
        "train_realized": y_train.mean(),
        "valid_realized": y_valid.mean(),
        "test_realized": y_test.mean()
    })

    hgb_models[i] = model

hgb_results = pd.DataFrame(hgb_results).sort_values(
    ["valid_auc", "valid_brier"],
    ascending=[False, True]
)

print("\nHIST GRADIENT BOOSTING RESULTS")
print(hgb_results)

best_model_id = int(hgb_results.iloc[0]["model_id"])
best_hgb = hgb_models[best_model_id]

print("\nBest HGB model_id:", best_model_id)
print(hgb_results.iloc[0])

# =========================================================
# 4. FINAL PERFORMANCE
# =========================================================
def evaluate_split(split_name, y, p):
    return {
        "split": split_name,
        "auc": roc_auc_score(y, p),
        "pr_auc": average_precision_score(y, p),
        "brier": brier_score_loss(y, p),
        "avg_pred_prob": p.mean(),
        "realized_distress_rate": y.mean()
    }

p_train = best_hgb.predict_proba(X_train_imp)[:, 1]
p_valid = best_hgb.predict_proba(X_valid_imp)[:, 1]
p_test  = best_hgb.predict_proba(X_test_imp)[:, 1]

hgb_eval = pd.DataFrame([
    evaluate_split("train", y_train, p_train),
    evaluate_split("valid", y_valid, p_valid),
    evaluate_split("test", y_test, p_test)
])

print("\nBEST HGB PERFORMANCE")
print(hgb_eval)

# =========================================================
# 5. TEST DECILE REPORT
# =========================================================
test_out = pd.DataFrame({
    "y_true": y_test.values,
    "p_hat": p_test
})

test_out["decile"] = pd.qcut(test_out["p_hat"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1

hgb_decile_report = (
    test_out.groupby("decile")
    .agg(
        obs=("y_true", "count"),
        distress_rate=("y_true", "mean"),
        avg_pred_prob=("p_hat", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("decile")
)

print("\nHGB TEST DECILE REPORT")
print(hgb_decile_report)

# =========================================================
# 6. TOP-DECILE CAPTURE
# =========================================================
def top_bucket_capture(y_true, p_hat, top_share=0.10):
    temp = pd.DataFrame({
        "y_true": np.asarray(y_true),
        "p_hat": np.asarray(p_hat)
    }).copy()

    cutoff = temp["p_hat"].quantile(1 - top_share)
    temp["top_bucket"] = (temp["p_hat"] >= cutoff).astype(int)

    total_events = temp["y_true"].sum()
    captured_events = temp.loc[temp["top_bucket"] == 1, "y_true"].sum()
    capture_rate = captured_events / total_events if total_events > 0 else np.nan

    return pd.DataFrame([{
        "top_share": top_share,
        "total_events": int(total_events),
        "captured_events": int(captured_events),
        "capture_rate": capture_rate
    }])

hgb_top_decile_capture = top_bucket_capture(y_test, p_test, top_share=0.10)
print("\nHGB TOP-DECILE CAPTURE")
print(hgb_top_decile_capture)

# =========================================================
# 7. ROC CURVE
# =========================================================
fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_test, p_test)
auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"HGB (AUC={auc:.4f})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("HistGradientBoosting ROC Curve - Test Sample")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 8. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

hgb_results.to_csv(output_dir + "hgb_tuning_results.csv", index=False)
hgb_eval.to_csv(output_dir + "hgb_eval_summary.csv", index=False)
hgb_decile_report.to_csv(output_dir + "hgb_test_decile_report.csv", index=False)
hgb_top_decile_capture.to_csv(output_dir + "hgb_top_decile_capture.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved files:")
print(output_dir + "hgb_tuning_results.csv")
print(output_dir + "hgb_eval_summary.csv")
print(output_dir + "hgb_test_decile_report.csv")
print(output_dir + "hgb_top_decile_capture.csv")

In [17]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, brier_score_loss, roc_curve

enet_grid = [
    {"C": 0.03, "l1_ratio": 0.10},
    {"C": 0.10, "l1_ratio": 0.20},
    {"C": 0.30, "l1_ratio": 0.30},
    {"C": 1.00, "l1_ratio": 0.50},
    {"C": 3.00, "l1_ratio": 0.70},
]

enet_results = []
enet_models = {}

for i, params in enumerate(enet_grid, start=1):
    model = Pipeline([
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
        ("clf", LogisticRegression(
            penalty="elasticnet",
            solver="saga",
            C=params["C"],
            l1_ratio=params["l1_ratio"],
            max_iter=5000,
            class_weight=None,
            random_state=42
        ))
    ])

    model.fit(X_train, y_train)

    p_train = model.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
    p_valid = model.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
    p_test  = model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

    enet_results.append({
        "model_id": i,
        "C": params["C"],
        "l1_ratio": params["l1_ratio"],
        "train_auc": roc_auc_score(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_auc": roc_auc_score(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_auc": roc_auc_score(y_test, p_test),
        "train_pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_test, p_test),
        "train_brier": brier_score_loss(y_train, p_train),
        "valid_brier": brier_score_loss(y_valid, p_valid),
        "test_brier": brier_score_loss(y_test, p_test),
        "train_avg_pred": p_train.mean(),
        "valid_avg_pred": p_valid.mean(),
        "test_avg_pred": p_test.mean(),
        "train_realized": y_train.mean(),
        "valid_realized": y_valid.mean(),
        "test_realized": y_test.mean()
    })

    enet_models[i] = model

enet_results = pd.DataFrame(enet_results).sort_values(
    ["valid_auc", "valid_brier"],
    ascending=[False, True]
)

print("\nELASTIC NET RESULTS")
print(enet_results)

best_model_id = int(enet_results.iloc[0]["model_id"])
best_enet = enet_models[best_model_id]

p_train = best_enet.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
p_valid = best_enet.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
p_test  = best_enet.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

enet_eval = pd.DataFrame([
    {"split": "train", "auc": roc_auc_score(y_train, p_train), "pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_train, p_train), "brier": brier_score_loss(y_train, p_train), "avg_pred_prob": p_train.mean(), "realized_distress_rate": y_train.mean()},
    {"split": "valid", "auc": roc_auc_score(y_valid, p_valid), "pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_valid, p_valid), "brier": brier_score_loss(y_valid, p_valid), "avg_pred_prob": p_valid.mean(), "realized_distress_rate": y_valid.mean()},
    {"split": "test",  "auc": roc_auc_score(y_test,  p_test),  "pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_test,  p_test),  "brier": brier_score_loss(y_test,  p_test),  "avg_pred_prob": p_test.mean(),  "realized_distress_rate": y_test.mean()}
])

print("\nBEST ELASTIC NET PERFORMANCE")
print(enet_eval)

In [18]:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# =========================================================
# 1. FINAL MODEL COMPARISON TABLE
# Fill these values with your final metrics
# =========================================================
comparison_df = pd.DataFrame([
    {
        "Model": "Ridge Logit",
        "Train_AUC": 0.8209,
        "Valid_AUC": 0.8086,
        "Test_AUC": 0.8045,
        "Test_PR_AUC": None,   # fill if available
        "Test_Brier": 0.02036,
        "Top_Decile_Capture": None,   # fill if available
    },
    {
        "Model": "Scorecard Logit",
        "Train_AUC": 0.8482,
        "Valid_AUC": 0.8313,
        "Test_AUC": 0.8132,
        "Test_PR_AUC": None,   # fill if available
        "Test_Brier": 0.01942,
        "Top_Decile_Capture": None,   # fill if available
    },
    {
        "Model": "Random Forest",
        "Train_AUC": 0.9965,
        "Valid_AUC": 0.8759,
        "Test_AUC": 0.8598,
        "Test_PR_AUC": None,   # fill if available
        "Test_Brier": 0.04005,
        "Top_Decile_Capture": 0.5830,
    },
    {
        "Model": "Hist Gradient Boosting",
        "Train_AUC": 0.9079,
        "Valid_AUC": 0.8784,
        "Test_AUC": 0.8569,
        "Test_PR_AUC": 0.1915,
        "Test_Brier": 0.01872,
        "Top_Decile_Capture": 0.5916,
    }
])

comparison_df["Overfit_Gap"] = comparison_df["Train_AUC"] - comparison_df["Test_AUC"]

comparison_df["Interpretation"] = [
    "Stable benchmark",
    "Best interpretable industry model",
    "Highest AUC but overfit",
    "Best overall balance"
]

print("\nFINAL MODEL COMPARISON")
print(comparison_df)

# Save table
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"
comparison_df.to_csv(output_dir + "final_model_comparison_table.csv", index=False)

# =========================================================
# 2. CHART: TEST AUC
# =========================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 5))
plt.bar(comparison_df["Model"], comparison_df["Test_AUC"])
plt.title("Test ROC-AUC by Model")
plt.ylabel("Test ROC-AUC")
plt.xticks(rotation=20)
plt.grid(True, axis="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 3. CHART: TEST BRIER
# =========================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 5))
plt.bar(comparison_df["Model"], comparison_df["Test_Brier"])
plt.title("Test Brier Score by Model")
plt.ylabel("Brier Score (Lower is Better)")
plt.xticks(rotation=20)
plt.grid(True, axis="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 4. CHART: OVERFITTING GAP
# =========================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 5))
plt.bar(comparison_df["Model"], comparison_df["Overfit_Gap"])
plt.title("Overfitting Gap (Train AUC - Test AUC)")
plt.ylabel("AUC Gap")
plt.xticks(rotation=20)
plt.grid(True, axis="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 5. CHART: TOP DECILE CAPTURE
# =========================================================
capture_df = comparison_df.dropna(subset=["Top_Decile_Capture"]).copy()

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.bar(capture_df["Model"], capture_df["Top_Decile_Capture"])
plt.title("Top-Decile Distress Capture")
plt.ylabel("Capture Rate")
plt.xticks(rotation=20)
plt.grid(True, axis="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\nSaved:")
print(output_dir + "final_model_comparison_table.csv")

FINAL MODEL COMPARISON
                    Model  Train_AUC  Valid_AUC  Test_AUC  Test_PR_AUC  \
0             Ridge Logit     0.8209     0.8086    0.8045          NaN   
1         Scorecard Logit     0.8482     0.8313    0.8132          NaN   
2           Random Forest     0.9965     0.8759    0.8598          NaN   
3  Hist Gradient Boosting     0.9079     0.8784    0.8569       0.1915   

   Test_Brier  Top_Decile_Capture  Overfit_Gap  \
0     0.02036                 NaN       0.0164   
1     0.01942                 NaN       0.0350   
2     0.04005              0.5830       0.1367   
3     0.01872              0.5916       0.0510   

                      Interpretation  
0                   Stable benchmark  
1  Best interpretable industry model  
2            Highest AUC but overfit  
3               Best overall balance  

Saved:
/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/final_model_comparison_table.csv
In [19]:
# Re-create the split before building the 2024 watchlist

# Option 1: if your notebook uses clean_df
if "clean_df" in globals():
    base_df = clean_df.copy()

# Option 2: if your notebook uses df
elif "df" in globals():
    base_df = df.copy()

else:
    raise NameError("Neither 'clean_df' nor 'df' exists. Run the data-prep/modeling cells first.")

# Make sure obs_year exists
if "obs_year" not in base_df.columns:
    if "obs_date" in base_df.columns:
        base_df["obs_year"] = pd.to_datetime(base_df["obs_date"]).dt.year
    else:
        raise KeyError("Need either 'obs_year' or 'obs_date' in the dataset.")

# Rebuild splits
train_df = base_df[base_df["obs_year"].between(2000, 2016)].copy()
valid_df = base_df[base_df["obs_year"].between(2017, 2020)].copy()
test_df  = base_df[base_df["obs_year"].between(2021, 2024)].copy()

print("Train:", train_df.shape)
print("Valid:", valid_df.shape)
print("Test :", test_df.shape)

Train: (297771, 72)
Valid: (56871, 72)
Test : (50768, 72)
In [20]:
# ============================================
# 2024 FORWARD-LOOKING RISK WATCHLIST
# ============================================

test_2024 = test_df[test_df["obs_year"] == 2024].copy()

print("2024 rows:", test_2024.shape[0])

target_col = "distress_next_4q"
if target_col in test_2024.columns:
    print("2024 realized distress rate:", test_2024[target_col].mean())

# Find available prediction columns
candidate_prob_cols = [
    "hgb_prob", "rf_prob", "scorecard_prob", "ridge_prob", "enet_prob",
    "pred_prob", "pred", "prob"
]

available_prob_cols = [c for c in candidate_prob_cols if c in test_2024.columns]
print("Available probability columns:", available_prob_cols)

if not available_prob_cols:
    raise KeyError(
        "No prediction probability column found. You need to run the model prediction cell first."
    )

# Choose the best available one
rank_col = "hgb_prob" if "hgb_prob" in test_2024.columns else available_prob_cols[0]
print("Using ranking column:", rank_col)

test_2024 = test_2024.sort_values(rank_col, ascending=False).copy()

test_2024["risk_percentile"] = test_2024[rank_col].rank(pct=True)
test_2024["risk_bucket"] = pd.cut(
    test_2024["risk_percentile"],
    bins=[0, 0.50, 0.80, 0.95, 1.00],
    labels=["Low", "Moderate", "High", "Severe"]
)

keep_cols = [c for c in [
    "permno", "obs_date", "obs_year", "distress_next_4q",
    rank_col, "risk_bucket",
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio",
    "curr_ratio", "inv_turn", "at_turn",
    "divyield", "ret", "vol", "sprtrn", "excess_ret", "log_shrout"
] if c in test_2024.columns]

watchlist_2024 = test_2024[keep_cols].copy()

print("\nTop 10 risky 2024 observations")
print(watchlist_2024.head(10))

bucket_summary = (
    watchlist_2024.groupby("risk_bucket", observed=False)
    .agg(
        obs=("permno", "count"),
        avg_pred=(rank_col, "mean"),
        realized_distress=("distress_next_4q", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
    if "distress_next_4q" in watchlist_2024.columns
    else
    watchlist_2024.groupby("risk_bucket", observed=False)
    .agg(
        obs=("permno", "count"),
        avg_pred=(rank_col, "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

print("\nBucket summary")
print(bucket_summary)

2024 rows: 3742
2024 realized distress rate: 0.03019775521111705
Available probability columns: []

In [21]:
# ============================================
# 2024 WATCHLIST FROM EXISTING HGB p_test
# ============================================

# Check required objects exist
needed = ["test_df", "p_test"]
for obj in needed:
    if obj not in globals():
        raise NameError(f"'{obj}' is not defined. Run the HGB model prediction cell first.")

# Copy test set and attach probabilities
test_scored = test_df.copy()
test_scored["hgb_prob"] = p_test

# Filter 2024
test_2024 = test_scored[test_scored["obs_year"] == 2024].copy()

print("2024 rows:", test_2024.shape[0])

if "distress_next_4q" in test_2024.columns:
    print("2024 realized distress rate:", test_2024["distress_next_4q"].mean())

# Rank highest risk first
rank_col = "hgb_prob"
test_2024 = test_2024.sort_values(rank_col, ascending=False).copy()

# Risk buckets
test_2024["risk_percentile"] = test_2024[rank_col].rank(pct=True)
test_2024["risk_bucket"] = pd.cut(
    test_2024["risk_percentile"],
    bins=[0, 0.50, 0.80, 0.95, 1.00],
    labels=["Low", "Moderate", "High", "Severe"]
)

keep_cols = [c for c in [
    "permno", "obs_date", "obs_year", "distress_next_4q",
    "hgb_prob", "risk_bucket",
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio",
    "curr_ratio", "inv_turn", "at_turn",
    "divyield", "ret", "vol", "sprtrn", "excess_ret", "log_shrout"
] if c in test_2024.columns]

watchlist_2024 = test_2024[keep_cols].copy()

print("\nTop 10 risky 2024 observations")
print(watchlist_2024.head(10))

bucket_summary = (
    watchlist_2024.groupby("risk_bucket", observed=False)
    .agg(
        obs=("permno", "count"),
        avg_pred=("hgb_prob", "mean"),
        realized_distress=("distress_next_4q", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

print("\nBucket summary")
print(bucket_summary)

# Save outputs
watchlist_2024.to_csv("watchlist_2024_hgb.csv", index=False)
bucket_summary.to_csv("watchlist_2024_hgb_bucket_summary.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved watchlist_2024_hgb.csv")
print("Saved watchlist_2024_hgb_bucket_summary.csv")

In [23]:
# Add company names/tickers to the 2024 watchlist

name_cols = [c for c in ["ticker", "comnam", "ncusip", "cusip"] if c in test_df.columns]

if len(name_cols) > 0:
    test_scored_named = test_scored.copy()
else:
    if "crsp" not in globals():
        raise NameError("Need 'crsp' in memory to map permno to company names. Run the data-loading cell first.")

    crsp_names = crsp.copy()

    # Build mkt_date if needed
    if "mkt_date" not in crsp_names.columns:
        if "date" in crsp_names.columns:
            crsp_names["mkt_date"] = pd.to_datetime(crsp_names["date"], errors="coerce")
        elif "qdate" in crsp_names.columns:
            crsp_names["mkt_date"] = pd.to_datetime(crsp_names["qdate"], errors="coerce")
        else:
            raise KeyError("crsp does not contain date/qdate for mapping names.")

    crsp_names["mkt_date"] = pd.to_datetime(crsp_names["mkt_date"], errors="coerce")

    name_keep = [c for c in ["permno", "mkt_date", "ticker", "comnam", "ncusip", "cusip"] if c in crsp_names.columns]
    crsp_names = crsp_names[name_keep].drop_duplicates()

    test_scored_named = test_scored.copy()
    if "obs_date" in test_scored_named.columns:
        test_scored_named["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(test_scored_named["obs_date"], errors="coerce")

    test_scored_named = test_scored_named.merge(
        crsp_names,
        left_on=["permno", "obs_date"],
        right_on=["permno", "mkt_date"],
        how="left"
    )

# Filter 2024 again after merge
watchlist_2024_named = test_scored_named[test_scored_named["obs_year"] == 2024].copy()

print(watchlist_2024_named[["permno", "obs_date"] + [c for c in ["ticker", "comnam"] if c in watchlist_2024_named.columns]].head(10))

        permno   obs_date ticker                       comnam

In [24]:
rank_col = "hgb_prob"

watchlist_2024_named = watchlist_2024_named.sort_values(rank_col, ascending=False).copy()

proof_cols = [c for c in [
    "permno", "ticker", "comnam", "obs_date",
    rank_col, "distress_next_4q",
    "bm", "npm", "roa", "roe", "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "vol", "excess_ret"
] if c in watchlist_2024_named.columns]

top100_proof = watchlist_2024_named[proof_cols].head(100).copy()

# make the label easier to read
top100_proof["actual_result"] = np.where(
    top100_proof["distress_next_4q"] == 1,
    "Actually distressed",
    "Did not distress"
)

print(top100_proof.head(25))

top100_proof.to_csv("top100_2024_proof_table.csv", index=False)
print("Saved top100_2024_proof_table.csv")

In [25]:
true_positives = top100_proof[top100_proof["distress_next_4q"] == 1].copy()
false_positives = top100_proof[top100_proof["distress_next_4q"] == 0].copy()

print("Top 100 predicted risky firms")
print("Actually distressed:", len(true_positives))
print("Did not distress   :", len(false_positives))

print("\nActually distressed firms among top 100:")
print(true_positives[[c for c in ["permno", "ticker", "comnam", "obs_date", "hgb_prob"] if c in true_positives.columns]].head(20))

print("\nDid not distress among top 100:")
print(false_positives[[c for c in ["permno", "ticker", "comnam", "obs_date", "hgb_prob"] if c in false_positives.columns]].head(20))

base_rate = watchlist_2024_named["distress_next_4q"].mean()

lift_table = pd.DataFrame({
    "group": ["Top 25", "Top 50", "Top 100", "Top 200", "All 2024"],
    "hit_rate": [
        watchlist_2024_named.head(25)["distress_next_4q"].mean(),
        watchlist_2024_named.head(50)["distress_next_4q"].mean(),
        watchlist_2024_named.head(100)["distress_next_4q"].mean(),
        watchlist_2024_named.head(200)["distress_next_4q"].mean(),
        base_rate
    ]
})

lift_table["lift_vs_base"] = lift_table["hit_rate"] / base_rate
print(lift_table)

lift_table.to_csv("lift_table_2024.csv", index=False)

n_not_distressed = int(len(top100) - n_distressed)

print("Top 100 predicted risky firms")

print("Did not distress:", n_not_distressed)

import pandas as pd
import os

# Set your saved file path
file_path = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/model_ready_credit_panel.csv"

# Load the saved model-ready dataset
df = pd.read_csv(file_path, low_memory=False)

# Basic date handling
df["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["obs_date"], errors="coerce")

# Check
print("Loaded shape:", df.shape)
print(df.head())
print(df.columns.tolist())

In [9]:
# =========================================================
# XGBOOST ON LOADED DATAFRAME: df
# =========================================================

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, brier_score_loss

# -----------------------------
# 1. BASIC PREP
# -----------------------------
data = df.copy()

data["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(data["obs_date"], errors="coerce")
data["obs_year"] = data["obs_date"].dt.year
target = "distress_next_4q"

data = data.dropna(subset=[target, "obs_year"]).copy()
data[target] = data[target].astype(int)

# -----------------------------
# 2. FEATURE SET
# Core features only first
# -----------------------------
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

# Keep only columns that actually exist
features = [c for c in features if c in data.columns]

print("Features used:")
print(features)

# -----------------------------
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# -----------------------------
train_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2000) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2014)].copy()
valid_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2015) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2018)].copy()
test_df  = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2019) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2024)].copy()

X_train = train_df[features]
y_train = train_df[target]

X_valid = valid_df[features]
y_valid = valid_df[target]

X_test = test_df[features]
y_test = test_df[target]

print("\nSplit summary")
print("Train:", X_train.shape, "Distress rate:", y_train.mean())
print("Valid:", X_valid.shape, "Distress rate:", y_valid.mean())
print("Test :", X_test.shape,  "Distress rate:", y_test.mean())

# -----------------------------
# 4. CLASS IMBALANCE
# -----------------------------
n_negative = (y_train == 0).sum()
n_positive = (y_train == 1).sum()
scale_pos_weight = n_negative / max(n_positive, 1)

print("\nscale_pos_weight:", round(scale_pos_weight, 4))

# -----------------------------
# 5. TRAIN XGBOOST
# XGBoost can handle NaNs directly
# -----------------------------
xgb_model = XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    eval_metric="logloss",
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1,
    scale_pos_weight=scale_pos_weight,
    max_depth=4,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    n_estimators=300,
    min_child_weight=5,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.8
)

xgb_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# -----------------------------
# 6. PREDICT
# -----------------------------
p_train = xgb_model.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1]
p_valid = xgb_model.predict_proba(X_valid)[:, 1]
p_test  = xgb_model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# -----------------------------
# 7. EVALUATION
# -----------------------------
def evaluate(y_true, p, name):
    print(f"\n{name}")
    print("ROC-AUC :", round(roc_auc_score(y_true, p), 4))
    print("PR-AUC  :", round(average_precision_score(y_true, p), 4))
    print("Brier   :", round(brier_score_loss(y_true, p), 4))

evaluate(y_train, p_train, "TRAIN")
evaluate(y_valid, p_valid, "VALID")
evaluate(y_test, p_test, "TEST")

# -----------------------------
# 8. FEATURE IMPORTANCE
# -----------------------------
importance_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": features,
    "importance": xgb_model.feature_importances_
}).sort_values("importance", ascending=False)

print("\nFeature importance")
print(importance_df)

# -----------------------------
# 9. TEST PREDICTIONS TABLE
# -----------------------------
test_results = test_df[["permno", "obs_date", target]].copy()
test_results["pred_pd_xgb"] = p_test
test_results["risk_decile_xgb"] = pd.qcut(
    test_results["pred_pd_xgb"],
    10,
    labels=False,
    duplicates="drop"
) + 1

decile_summary = (
    test_results.groupby("risk_decile_xgb")
    .agg(
        obs_count=(target, "count"),
        realized_distress_rate=(target, "mean"),
        avg_pred_pd=("pred_pd_xgb", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

print("\nTest decile summary")
print(decile_summary)

# -----------------------------
# 10. SAVE OUTPUTS
# -----------------------------
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

importance_df.to_csv(output_dir + "xgb_feature_importance_simple.csv", index=False)
test_results.to_csv(output_dir + "xgb_test_predictions_simple.csv", index=False)
decile_summary.to_csv(output_dir + "xgb_test_deciles_simple.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved:")
print(output_dir + "xgb_feature_importance_simple.csv")
print(output_dir + "xgb_test_predictions_simple.csv")
print(output_dir + "xgb_test_deciles_simple.csv")

Features used:
['bm', 'npm', 'gpm', 'roa', 'roe', 'gprof', 'invt_act', 'rect_act', 'curr_debt', 'debt_capital', 'de_ratio', 'quick_ratio', 'curr_ratio', 'at_turn', 'ret', 'excess_ret', 'vol', 'shrout']

/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/xgb_feature_importance_simple.csv
/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/xgb_test_predictions_simple.csv
/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/xgb_test_deciles_simple.csv
In [10]:
# =========================================================
# CALIBRATED XGBOOST VS LOGISTIC BASELINE
# Uses loaded dataframe: df
# =========================================================

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.calibration import CalibratedClassifierCV, calibration_curve
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, brier_score_loss
from xgboost import XGBClassifier

# =========================================================
# 1. BASIC PREP
# =========================================================
data = df.copy()

data["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(data["obs_date"], errors="coerce")
data["obs_year"] = data["obs_date"].dt.year
target = "distress_next_4q"

data = data.dropna(subset=[target, "obs_year"]).copy()
data[target] = data[target].astype(int)

# =========================================================
# 2. FEATURE SET
# Same core set used in your XGBoost run
# =========================================================
features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

features = [c for c in features if c in data.columns]

print("Features used:")
print(features)

# =========================================================
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# Train / calibration / test
# We use:
# Train: 2000-2014
# Calibration set: 2015-2018
# Test: 2019-2024
# =========================================================
train_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2000) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2014)].copy()
calib_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2015) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2018)].copy()
test_df  = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2019) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2024)].copy()

X_train = train_df[features]
y_train = train_df[target]

X_calib = calib_df[features]
y_calib = calib_df[target]

X_test = test_df[features]
y_test = test_df[target]

print("\nSplit summary")
print("Train:", X_train.shape, "Distress rate:", y_train.mean())
print("Calib:", X_calib.shape, "Distress rate:", y_calib.mean())
print("Test :", X_test.shape,  "Distress rate:", y_test.mean())

# =========================================================
# 4. LOGISTIC BASELINE
# =========================================================
logit_model = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(
        max_iter=3000,
        class_weight="balanced",
        solver="liblinear"
    ))
])

logit_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

p_logit_calib = logit_model.predict_proba(X_calib)[:, 1]
p_logit_test  = logit_model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 5. XGBOOST BASE MODEL
# =========================================================
n_negative = (y_train == 0).sum()
n_positive = (y_train == 1).sum()
scale_pos_weight = n_negative / max(n_positive, 1)

xgb_base = XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    eval_metric="logloss",
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1,
    scale_pos_weight=scale_pos_weight,
    max_depth=4,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    n_estimators=300,
    min_child_weight=5,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.8
)

xgb_base.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Raw XGBoost predictions
p_xgb_raw_calib = xgb_base.predict_proba(X_calib)[:, 1]
p_xgb_raw_test  = xgb_base.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 6. CALIBRATE XGBOOST
# method can be "sigmoid" or "isotonic"
# sigmoid is safer when calibration set is not huge
# =========================================================
xgb_calibrated = CalibratedClassifierCV(
    estimator=xgb_base,
    method="sigmoid",
    cv="prefit"
)

xgb_calibrated.fit(X_calib, y_calib)

p_xgb_calib_calib = xgb_calibrated.predict_proba(X_calib)[:, 1]
p_xgb_calib_test  = xgb_calibrated.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 7. METRIC FUNCTIONS
# =========================================================
def metric_table(y_true, p, model_name, split_name):
    return {
        "model": model_name,
        "split": split_name,
        "roc_auc": roc_auc_score(y_true, p),
        "pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_true, p),
        "brier_score": brier_score_loss(y_true, p)
    }

def top_decile_summary(y_true, p, model_name, split_name):
    tmp = pd.DataFrame({"y": y_true.values, "p": p})
    tmp["decile"] = pd.qcut(tmp["p"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1
    top_decile = tmp["decile"].max()

    top_rate = tmp.loc[tmp["decile"] == top_decile, "y"].mean()
    distress_in_top = ((tmp["decile"] == top_decile) & (tmp["y"] == 1)).sum()
    total_distress = (tmp["y"] == 1).sum()
    capture_share = distress_in_top / max(total_distress, 1)

    return {
        "model": model_name,
        "split": split_name,
        "top_decile_realized_distress_rate": top_rate,
        "share_of_all_distress_in_top_decile": capture_share
    }

# =========================================================
# 8. BUILD COMPARISON TABLES
# =========================================================
metrics = []

metrics.append(metric_table(y_calib, p_logit_calib,     "Logistic",        "Calibration"))
metrics.append(metric_table(y_test,  p_logit_test,      "Logistic",        "Test"))

metrics.append(metric_table(y_calib, p_xgb_raw_calib,   "XGBoost Raw",     "Calibration"))
metrics.append(metric_table(y_test,  p_xgb_raw_test,    "XGBoost Raw",     "Test"))

metrics.append(metric_table(y_calib, p_xgb_calib_calib, "XGBoost Calibr.", "Calibration"))
metrics.append(metric_table(y_test,  p_xgb_calib_test,  "XGBoost Calibr.", "Test"))

metrics_df = pd.DataFrame(metrics)

top_deciles = []
top_deciles.append(top_decile_summary(y_test, p_logit_test,      "Logistic",        "Test"))
top_deciles.append(top_decile_summary(y_test, p_xgb_raw_test,    "XGBoost Raw",     "Test"))
top_deciles.append(top_decile_summary(y_test, p_xgb_calib_test,  "XGBoost Calibr.", "Test"))

top_deciles_df = pd.DataFrame(top_deciles)

print("\nMODEL PERFORMANCE COMPARISON")
print(metrics_df)

print("\nTOP DECILE COMPARISON")
print(top_deciles_df)

# =========================================================
# 9. CALIBRATION PLOT ON TEST SET
# =========================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))

for probs, label in [
    (p_logit_test, "Logistic"),
    (p_xgb_raw_test, "XGBoost Raw"),
    (p_xgb_calib_test, "XGBoost Calibrated")
]:
    frac_pos, mean_pred = calibration_curve(y_test, probs, n_bins=10, strategy="quantile")
    plt.plot(mean_pred, frac_pos, marker="o", label=label)

# Perfect calibration line
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")

plt.xlabel("Mean Predicted Probability")
plt.ylabel("Observed Distress Rate")
plt.title("Calibration Plot (Test Set)")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 10. SAVE TEST PREDICTIONS
# =========================================================
test_predictions = test_df[["permno", "obs_date", target]].copy()
test_predictions["pd_logistic"] = p_logit_test
test_predictions["pd_xgb_raw"] = p_xgb_raw_test
test_predictions["pd_xgb_calibrated"] = p_xgb_calib_test

output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

metrics_df.to_csv(output_dir + "model_comparison_calibration.csv", index=False)
top_deciles_df.to_csv(output_dir + "top_decile_comparison.csv", index=False)
test_predictions.to_csv(output_dir + "test_predictions_with_calibration.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved:")
print(output_dir + "model_comparison_calibration.csv")
print(output_dir + "top_decile_comparison.csv")
print(output_dir + "test_predictions_with_calibration.csv")

Features used:
['bm', 'npm', 'gpm', 'roa', 'roe', 'gprof', 'invt_act', 'rect_act', 'curr_debt', 'debt_capital', 'de_ratio', 'quick_ratio', 'curr_ratio', 'at_turn', 'ret', 'excess_ret', 'vol', 'shrout']

Calib: (58026, 18) Distress rate: 0.01607899906938269

In [12]:
# =========================================================
# SECOND XGBOOST WITH LAG/CHANGE FEATURES + SHAP
# =========================================================
!pip install --upgrade pip
!pip install shap

import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from xgboost import XGBClassifier
from sklearn.calibration import CalibratedClassifierCV
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, brier_score_loss

# Optional install if needed:
# !pip install shap

import shap

# =========================================================
# 1. PREP DATA
# =========================================================
data = df.copy()

data["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(data["obs_date"], errors="coerce")
data["obs_year"] = data["obs_date"].dt.year
target = "distress_next_4q"

data = data.dropna(subset=[target, "obs_year"]).copy()
data[target] = data[target].astype(int)

# =========================================================
# 2. FEATURE SETS
# =========================================================
base_features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

lag_change_features = [
    "roa_lag1", "roa_chg1",
    "roe_lag1", "roe_chg1",
    "npm_lag1", "npm_chg1",
    "gpm_lag1", "gpm_chg1",
    "de_ratio_lag1", "de_ratio_chg1",
    "quick_ratio_lag1", "quick_ratio_chg1",
    "curr_ratio_lag1", "curr_ratio_chg1"
]

base_features = [c for c in base_features if c in data.columns]
lag_change_features = [c for c in lag_change_features if c in data.columns]

features_v2 = base_features + lag_change_features
features_v2 = list(dict.fromkeys(features_v2))

print("Base features:", len(base_features))
print("Lag/change features:", len(lag_change_features))
print("Total features in model v2:", len(features_v2))
print(features_v2)

# =========================================================
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# =========================================================
train_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2000) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2014)].copy()
calib_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2015) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2018)].copy()
test_df  = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2019) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2024)].copy()

X_train = train_df[features_v2]
y_train = train_df[target]

X_calib = calib_df[features_v2]
y_calib = calib_df[target]

X_test = test_df[features_v2]
y_test = test_df[target]

print("\nSplit summary")
print("Train:", X_train.shape, "Distress rate:", y_train.mean())
print("Calib:", X_calib.shape, "Distress rate:", y_calib.mean())
print("Test :", X_test.shape,  "Distress rate:", y_test.mean())

# =========================================================
# 4. CLASS IMBALANCE
# =========================================================
n_negative = (y_train == 0).sum()
n_positive = (y_train == 1).sum()
scale_pos_weight = n_negative / max(n_positive, 1)

print("\nscale_pos_weight:", round(scale_pos_weight, 4))

# =========================================================
# 5. SECOND XGBOOST MODEL
# Slightly more conservative than before
# =========================================================
xgb_v2 = XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    eval_metric="logloss",
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1,
    scale_pos_weight=scale_pos_weight,
    max_depth=4,
    learning_rate=0.03,
    n_estimators=400,
    min_child_weight=8,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.8,
    reg_lambda=1.0
)

xgb_v2.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Raw probabilities
p_v2_calib_raw = xgb_v2.predict_proba(X_calib)[:, 1]
p_v2_test_raw  = xgb_v2.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 6. CALIBRATE SECOND XGBOOST
# =========================================================
xgb_v2_calibrated = CalibratedClassifierCV(
    estimator=xgb_v2,
    method="sigmoid",
    cv="prefit"
)

xgb_v2_calibrated.fit(X_calib, y_calib)

p_v2_calib = xgb_v2_calibrated.predict_proba(X_calib)[:, 1]
p_v2_test  = xgb_v2_calibrated.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 7. EVALUATION FUNCTIONS
# =========================================================
def evaluate(y_true, p, name):
    return {
        "model": name,
        "roc_auc": roc_auc_score(y_true, p),
        "pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_true, p),
        "brier_score": brier_score_loss(y_true, p)
    }

def top_decile_capture(y_true, p):
    tmp = pd.DataFrame({"y": y_true.values, "p": p})
    tmp["decile"] = pd.qcut(tmp["p"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1
    top_decile = tmp["decile"].max()

    top_rate = tmp.loc[tmp["decile"] == top_decile, "y"].mean()
    distress_in_top = ((tmp["decile"] == top_decile) & (tmp["y"] == 1)).sum()
    total_distress = (tmp["y"] == 1).sum()
    capture_share = distress_in_top / max(total_distress, 1)

    return {
        "top_decile_realized_distress_rate": top_rate,
        "share_of_all_distress_in_top_decile": capture_share
    }

# =========================================================
# 8. METRICS
# =========================================================
metrics_df = pd.DataFrame([
    {"split": "Calibration", **evaluate(y_calib, p_v2_calib_raw, "XGBoost V2 Raw")},
    {"split": "Test",        **evaluate(y_test,  p_v2_test_raw,  "XGBoost V2 Raw")},
    {"split": "Calibration", **evaluate(y_calib, p_v2_calib,     "XGBoost V2 Calibrated")},
    {"split": "Test",        **evaluate(y_test,  p_v2_test,      "XGBoost V2 Calibrated")},
])

print("\nSECOND XGBOOST RESULTS")
print(metrics_df)

top_decile_df = pd.DataFrame([
    {"model": "XGBoost V2 Raw", "split": "Test", **top_decile_capture(y_test, p_v2_test_raw)},
    {"model": "XGBoost V2 Calibrated", "split": "Test", **top_decile_capture(y_test, p_v2_test)},
])

print("\nTOP DECILE RESULTS")
print(top_decile_df)

# =========================================================
# 9. FEATURE IMPORTANCE
# =========================================================
importance_df_v2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": features_v2,
    "importance": xgb_v2.feature_importances_
}).sort_values("importance", ascending=False)

print("\nTop feature importance (V2)")
print(importance_df_v2.head(25))

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
top_n = min(20, len(importance_df_v2))
plt.barh(
    importance_df_v2["feature"].head(top_n)[::-1],
    importance_df_v2["importance"].head(top_n)[::-1]
)
plt.xlabel("Importance")
plt.ylabel("Feature")
plt.title("XGBoost V2 Feature Importances")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 10. SHAP EXPLANATIONS
# Use raw xgb model, not calibrated wrapper
# =========================================================
# Take a manageable sample for SHAP speed
shap_sample_size = min(2000, len(X_test))
X_test_sample = X_test.sample(shap_sample_size, random_state=42)

explainer = shap.TreeExplainer(xgb_v2)
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_test_sample)

# Summary plot
shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_test_sample, plot_type="bar", show=True)
shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_test_sample, show=True)

# =========================================================
# 11. EXPLAIN A FEW INDIVIDUAL FIRMS
# =========================================================
# Choose 3 highest-risk test observations based on calibrated probability
test_predictions_v2 = test_df[["permno", "obs_date", target]].copy()
test_predictions_v2["pd_xgb_v2_raw"] = p_v2_test_raw
test_predictions_v2["pd_xgb_v2_calibrated"] = p_v2_test

top_cases = test_predictions_v2.sort_values("pd_xgb_v2_calibrated", ascending=False).head(3)
print("\nTop 3 highest-risk test cases")
print(top_cases)

top_case_indices = top_cases.index.tolist()
X_top_cases = X_test.loc[top_case_indices]

# Waterfall-style explanations
for idx in X_top_cases.index:
    print(f"\nSHAP explanation for index {idx}, permno={test_df.loc[idx, 'permno']}, obs_date={test_df.loc[idx, 'obs_date']}")
    shap.plots.waterfall(
        shap.Explanation(
            values=explainer.shap_values(X_test.loc[[idx]])[0],
            base_values=explainer.expected_value,
            data=X_test.loc[idx].values,
            feature_names=X_test.columns.tolist()
        ),
        max_display=12
    )

# =========================================================
# 12. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"

metrics_df.to_csv(output_dir + "xgb_v2_metrics.csv", index=False)
top_decile_df.to_csv(output_dir + "xgb_v2_top_decile.csv", index=False)
importance_df_v2.to_csv(output_dir + "xgb_v2_feature_importance.csv", index=False)
test_predictions_v2.to_csv(output_dir + "xgb_v2_test_predictions.csv", index=False)

print("\nSaved:")
print(output_dir + "xgb_v2_metrics.csv")
print(output_dir + "xgb_v2_top_decile.csv")
print(output_dir + "xgb_v2_feature_importance.csv")
print(output_dir + "xgb_v2_test_predictions.csv")

/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/xgb_v2_top_decile.csv

In [13]:
# =========================================================
# FULL SIMPLE TEXTUAL ANALYSIS / SIMPLE RAG PIPELINE
# CREDIT DISTRESS MODEL + CALIBRATION + SHAP + TF-IDF RETRIEVAL
#
# WHAT THIS DOES
# 1. Uses your loaded dataframe: df
# 2. Trains XGBoost V2 with base + lag/change features
# 3. Calibrates predicted probabilities
# 4. Computes SHAP values on the test set
# 5. Creates one text document per firm-quarter
# 6. Builds a TF-IDF retriever over those documents
# 7. Lets you query similar risky cases in plain English
# 8. Saves outputs
#
# BEFORE RUNNING
# - Make sure df is already loaded
# - If needed:
#   !pip install xgboost shap scikit-learn
# =========================================================

import os
import re
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import warnings

from xgboost import XGBClassifier
from sklearn.calibration import CalibratedClassifierCV
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, brier_score_loss
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity

import shap

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# =========================================================
# 1. PREP DATA
# =========================================================
data = df.copy()

data["obs_date"] = pd.to_datetime(data["obs_date"], errors="coerce")
data["obs_year"] = data["obs_date"].dt.year
target = "distress_next_4q"

data = data.dropna(subset=[target, "obs_year"]).copy()
data[target] = data[target].astype(int)

print("Dataset shape:", data.shape)
print("Target rate:", round(data[target].mean(), 6))

# =========================================================
# 2. DEFINE FEATURES
# =========================================================
base_features = [
    "bm", "npm", "gpm", "roa", "roe", "gprof",
    "invt_act", "rect_act", "curr_debt",
    "debt_capital", "de_ratio", "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio",
    "at_turn", "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout"
]

lag_change_features = [
    "roa_lag1", "roa_chg1",
    "roe_lag1", "roe_chg1",
    "npm_lag1", "npm_chg1",
    "gpm_lag1", "gpm_chg1",
    "de_ratio_lag1", "de_ratio_chg1",
    "quick_ratio_lag1", "quick_ratio_chg1",
    "curr_ratio_lag1", "curr_ratio_chg1"
]

base_features = [c for c in base_features if c in data.columns]
lag_change_features = [c for c in lag_change_features if c in data.columns]

features_v2 = base_features + lag_change_features
features_v2 = list(dict.fromkeys(features_v2))

print("\nBase features:", len(base_features))
print("Lag/change features:", len(lag_change_features))
print("Total model features:", len(features_v2))
print(features_v2)

# =========================================================
# 3. TIME SPLIT
# =========================================================
train_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2000) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2014)].copy()
calib_df = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2015) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2018)].copy()
test_df  = data[(data["obs_year"] >= 2019) & (data["obs_year"] <= 2024)].copy()

X_train = train_df[features_v2]
y_train = train_df[target]

X_calib = calib_df[features_v2]
y_calib = calib_df[target]

X_test = test_df[features_v2]
y_test = test_df[target]

print("\nSplit summary")
print("Train:", X_train.shape, "Distress rate:", round(y_train.mean(), 6))
print("Calib:", X_calib.shape, "Distress rate:", round(y_calib.mean(), 6))
print("Test :", X_test.shape,  "Distress rate:", round(y_test.mean(), 6))

# =========================================================
# 4. CLASS IMBALANCE
# =========================================================
n_negative = (y_train == 0).sum()
n_positive = (y_train == 1).sum()
scale_pos_weight = n_negative / max(n_positive, 1)

print("\nscale_pos_weight:", round(scale_pos_weight, 4))

# =========================================================
# 5. TRAIN XGBOOST V2
# =========================================================
xgb_v2 = XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    eval_metric="logloss",
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1,
    scale_pos_weight=scale_pos_weight,
    max_depth=4,
    learning_rate=0.03,
    n_estimators=400,
    min_child_weight=8,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.8,
    reg_lambda=1.0
)

xgb_v2.fit(X_train, y_train)

p_v2_calib_raw = xgb_v2.predict_proba(X_calib)[:, 1]
p_v2_test_raw  = xgb_v2.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 6. CALIBRATE XGBOOST V2
# =========================================================
xgb_v2_calibrated = CalibratedClassifierCV(
    estimator=xgb_v2,
    method="sigmoid",
    cv="prefit"
)

xgb_v2_calibrated.fit(X_calib, y_calib)

p_v2_calib = xgb_v2_calibrated.predict_proba(X_calib)[:, 1]
p_v2_test  = xgb_v2_calibrated.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# =========================================================
# 7. EVALUATION FUNCTIONS
# =========================================================
def evaluate(y_true, p):
    return {
        "roc_auc": roc_auc_score(y_true, p),
        "pr_auc": average_precision_score(y_true, p),
        "brier_score": brier_score_loss(y_true, p)
    }

def top_decile_capture(y_true, p):
    tmp = pd.DataFrame({"y": y_true.values, "p": p})
    tmp["decile"] = pd.qcut(tmp["p"], 10, labels=False, duplicates="drop") + 1
    top_decile = tmp["decile"].max()

    top_rate = tmp.loc[tmp["decile"] == top_decile, "y"].mean()
    distress_in_top = ((tmp["decile"] == top_decile) & (tmp["y"] == 1)).sum()
    total_distress = (tmp["y"] == 1).sum()
    capture_share = distress_in_top / max(total_distress, 1)

    return {
        "top_decile_realized_distress_rate": top_rate,
        "share_of_all_distress_in_top_decile": capture_share
    }

# =========================================================
# 8. PRINT MODEL RESULTS
# =========================================================
metrics_df = pd.DataFrame([
    {"split": "Calibration", "model": "XGBoost V2 Raw", **evaluate(y_calib, p_v2_calib_raw)},
    {"split": "Test",        "model": "XGBoost V2 Raw", **evaluate(y_test,  p_v2_test_raw)},
    {"split": "Calibration", "model": "XGBoost V2 Calibrated", **evaluate(y_calib, p_v2_calib)},
    {"split": "Test",        "model": "XGBoost V2 Calibrated", **evaluate(y_test,  p_v2_test)},
])

top_decile_df = pd.DataFrame([
    {"split": "Test", "model": "XGBoost V2 Raw", **top_decile_capture(y_test, p_v2_test_raw)},
    {"split": "Test", "model": "XGBoost V2 Calibrated", **top_decile_capture(y_test, p_v2_test)},
])

print("\nMODEL RESULTS")
print(metrics_df)

print("\nTOP DECILE RESULTS")
print(top_decile_df)

# =========================================================
# 9. CALIBRATION PLOT
# =========================================================
from sklearn.calibration import calibration_curve

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))

for probs, label in [
    (p_v2_test_raw, "XGBoost V2 Raw"),
    (p_v2_test, "XGBoost V2 Calibrated")
]:
    frac_pos, mean_pred = calibration_curve(y_test, probs, n_bins=10, strategy="quantile")
    plt.plot(mean_pred, frac_pos, marker="o", label=label)

plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("Mean Predicted Probability")
plt.ylabel("Observed Distress Rate")
plt.title("Calibration Plot (Test Set)")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =========================================================
# 10. SHAP VALUES ON TEST SET
# =========================================================
print("\nComputing SHAP values...")

explainer = shap.TreeExplainer(xgb_v2)

# Use a manageable sample for global summary
shap_sample_size = min(2000, len(X_test))
X_test_sample = X_test.sample(shap_sample_size, random_state=42)

shap_values_sample = explainer.shap_values(X_test_sample)

# Global SHAP bar plot
shap.summary_plot(shap_values_sample, X_test_sample, plot_type="bar", show=True)

# Global SHAP beeswarm
shap.summary_plot(shap_values_sample, X_test_sample, show=True)

# =========================================================
# 11. TEST PREDICTION TABLE
# =========================================================
test_results = test_df[["permno", "obs_date", target]].copy()
test_results["pd_xgb_v2_raw"] = p_v2_test_raw
test_results["pd_xgb_v2_calibrated"] = p_v2_test
test_results["risk_decile_xgb_v2"] = pd.qcut(
    test_results["pd_xgb_v2_calibrated"],
    10,
    labels=False,
    duplicates="drop"
) + 1

# attach feature columns too
for col in features_v2:
    test_results[col] = X_test[col].values

# =========================================================
# 12. HELPER FUNCTIONS FOR TEXT GENERATION
# =========================================================
def fmt_num(x, digits=3):
    if pd.isna(x):
        return "missing"
    if isinstance(x, (int, np.integer)):
        return str(x)
    if isinstance(x, (float, np.floating)):
        return f"{x:.{digits}f}"
    return str(x)

def risk_bucket(pd_value):
    if pd_value >= 0.20:
        return "very high"
    elif pd_value >= 0.10:
        return "high"
    elif pd_value >= 0.05:
        return "moderate"
    else:
        return "low"

def distress_flag_text(y):
    return "distress event occurred within the next 4 quarters" if y == 1 else "no distress event occurred within the next 4 quarters"

# =========================================================
# 13. GET TOP SHAP DRIVERS FOR EACH TEST ROW
# =========================================================
print("\nComputing SHAP values for all test rows...")
shap_values_test = explainer.shap_values(X_test)

# If shap returns list, use first element for binary classification compatibility
if isinstance(shap_values_test, list):
    shap_values_test = shap_values_test[0]

def top_shap_drivers_for_row(row_idx, feature_names, shap_values_matrix, top_n=5):
    vals = shap_values_matrix[row_idx]
    pairs = list(zip(feature_names, vals))
    pairs_sorted = sorted(pairs, key=lambda x: abs(x[1]), reverse=True)[:top_n]

    driver_list = []
    for feat, shap_val in pairs_sorted:
        direction = "increased" if shap_val > 0 else "reduced"
        driver_list.append((feat, shap_val, direction))
    return driver_list

# =========================================================
# 14. BUILD TEXT DOCUMENT FOR EACH FIRM-QUARTER
# =========================================================
def make_document(row, row_idx):
    pd_value = row["pd_xgb_v2_calibrated"]
    bucket = risk_bucket(pd_value)
    outcome_text = distress_flag_text(int(row[target]))

    # Selected key fields for readable narrative
    selected_fields = [
        "roe", "npm", "roa", "gpm", "bm",
        "quick_ratio", "curr_ratio", "debt_capital",
        "ret", "excess_ret", "vol", "shrout",
        "roe_chg1", "npm_chg1", "roa_chg1",
        "quick_ratio_lag1", "de_ratio_lag1"
    ]
    selected_fields = [c for c in selected_fields if c in row.index]

    field_text = []
    for c in selected_fields:
        field_text.append(f"{c}={fmt_num(row[c])}")

    drivers = top_shap_drivers_for_row(
        row_idx=row_idx,
        feature_names=features_v2,
        shap_values_matrix=shap_values_test,
        top_n=5
    )

    driver_sentences = []
    for feat, shap_val, direction in drivers:
        driver_sentences.append(
            f"{feat} {direction} model risk with shap={fmt_num(shap_val, digits=3)} and value={fmt_num(row[feat])}"
        )

    doc = (
        f"Firm-quarter risk profile. "
        f"permno {row['permno']}. "
        f"observation date {pd.to_datetime(row['obs_date']).date()}. "
        f"calibrated predicted distress probability {fmt_num(pd_value, digits=4)}. "
        f"risk bucket {bucket}. "
        f"risk decile {row['risk_decile_xgb_v2']}. "
        f"actual future outcome: {outcome_text}. "
        f"key financial indicators: {'; '.join(field_text)}. "
        f"top shap drivers: {'; '.join(driver_sentences)}."
    )
    return doc

print("\nBuilding text documents...")

test_results = test_results.reset_index(drop=True)
X_test_reset = X_test.reset_index(drop=True)

documents = []
for i in range(len(test_results)):
    merged_row = pd.concat([test_results.loc[i], X_test_reset.loc[i]])
    # remove duplicate labels if any
    merged_row = merged_row[~merged_row.index.duplicated(keep="first")]
    documents.append(make_document(merged_row, i))

test_results["rag_text"] = documents

print("\nExample document:")
print(test_results["rag_text"].iloc[0][:1000])

# =========================================================
# 15. BUILD SIMPLE TF-IDF RETRIEVAL INDEX
# =========================================================
print("\nBuilding TF-IDF retriever...")

vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
    stop_words="english",
    ngram_range=(1, 2),
    min_df=2,
    max_features=5000
)

doc_matrix = vectorizer.fit_transform(test_results["rag_text"])

print("Document matrix shape:", doc_matrix.shape)

# =========================================================
# 16. SIMPLE RETRIEVAL FUNCTION
# =========================================================
def retrieve_cases(query, top_k=5):
    """
    Returns top_k most relevant firm-quarter documents for a natural-language query.
    """
    query_vec = vectorizer.transform([query])
    sims = cosine_similarity(query_vec, doc_matrix).flatten()
    top_idx = np.argsort(-sims)[:top_k]

    out = test_results.loc[top_idx, [
        "permno", "obs_date", target,
        "pd_xgb_v2_calibrated", "risk_decile_xgb_v2", "rag_text"
    ]].copy()

    out["similarity_score"] = sims[top_idx]
    out = out.sort_values("similarity_score", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
    return out

# =========================================================
# 17. OPTIONAL SUMMARY FUNCTION
# This is not a true LLM generation step.
# It gives a simple readable summary from retrieved cases.
# =========================================================
def answer_query_simple(query, top_k=5):
    retrieved = retrieve_cases(query, top_k=top_k)

    print("\n=================================================")
    print("QUERY")
    print(query)
    print("=================================================\n")

    print("TOP MATCHES\n")
    for i, row in retrieved.iterrows():
        print(f"Rank {i+1}")
        print(f"permno: {row['permno']}")
        print(f"obs_date: {row['obs_date']}")
        print(f"predicted_pd: {row['pd_xgb_v2_calibrated']:.4f}")
        print(f"risk_decile: {row['risk_decile_xgb_v2']}")
        print(f"actual_distress: {row[target]}")
        print(f"similarity_score: {row['similarity_score']:.4f}")
        print(f"text: {row['rag_text']}")
        print("-" * 80)

    return retrieved

# =========================================================
# 18. EXAMPLE QUERIES
# =========================================================
example_queries = [
    "high distress risk with weak liquidity and negative returns",
    "firms with worsening profitability and high leverage",
    "cases where roe and npm deteriorated and distress risk is high",
    "very high risk firms with low quick ratio and poor excess return",
    "distressed firms similar to low roe and weak cash position"
]

for q in example_queries:
    _ = answer_query_simple(q, top_k=3)

# =========================================================
# 19. OPTIONAL: EXPLAIN A SPECIFIC PERMNO
# =========================================================
def explain_permno(permno_value, top_n=5):
    subset = test_results[test_results["permno"] == permno_value].copy()
    if subset.empty:
        print(f"No test observations found for permno {permno_value}")
        return None

    subset = subset.sort_values("pd_xgb_v2_calibrated", ascending=False).head(top_n)
    print(f"\nTop {len(subset)} highest-risk test observations for permno {permno_value}\n")
    for _, row in subset.iterrows():
        print(f"obs_date: {row['obs_date']}")
        print(f"predicted_pd: {row['pd_xgb_v2_calibrated']:.4f}")
        print(f"risk_decile: {row['risk_decile_xgb_v2']}")
        print(f"actual_distress: {row[target]}")
        print(f"text: {row['rag_text']}")
        print("-" * 80)
    return subset

# Example:
# explain_permno(13544)

# =========================================================
# 20. OPTIONAL: LOCAL SHAP WATERFALL FOR TOP 3 RISKY CASES
# =========================================================
print("\nTop 3 highest-risk test cases")
top_cases = test_results.sort_values("pd_xgb_v2_calibrated", ascending=False).head(3)
print(top_cases[["permno", "obs_date", target, "pd_xgb_v2_raw", "pd_xgb_v2_calibrated"]])

for idx in top_cases.index.tolist():
    print(f"\nSHAP explanation for index {idx}, permno={test_results.loc[idx, 'permno']}, obs_date={test_results.loc[idx, 'obs_date']}")
    shap_explanation = shap.Explanation(
        values=explainer.shap_values(X_test_reset.loc[[idx]])[0],
        base_values=explainer.expected_value,
        data=X_test_reset.loc[idx].values,
        feature_names=X_test_reset.columns.tolist()
    )
    shap.plots.waterfall(shap_explanation, max_display=12)

# =========================================================
# 21. SAVE OUTPUTS
# =========================================================
output_dir = "/Users/nickythakurathi/Desktop/Dr. Ishita/Independent Study Dataset/"
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

metrics_df.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_xgb_v2_metrics.csv"), index=False)
top_decile_df.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_xgb_v2_top_decile.csv"), index=False)
test_results.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_test_documents.csv"), index=False)

# Save TF-IDF vocabulary for reuse
vocab_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "term": vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()
})
vocab_df.to_csv(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_tfidf_vocabulary.csv"), index=False)

print("\nSaved files:")
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_xgb_v2_metrics.csv"))
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_xgb_v2_top_decile.csv"))
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_test_documents.csv"))
print(os.path.join(output_dir, "simple_rag_tfidf_vocabulary.csv"))

firms with worsening profitability and high leverage
=================================================

permno: 16793

predicted_pd: 0.2063
risk_decile: 10
actual_distress: 0
similarity_score: 0.0916
text: Firm-quarter risk profile. permno 16793. observation date 2023-04-30. calibrated predicted distress probability 0.2063. risk bucket very high. risk decile 10. actual future outcome: no distress event occurred within the next 4 quarters. key financial indicators: roe=-1.465; npm=missing; roa=-1.179; gpm=missing; bm=0.559; quick_ratio=3.454; curr_ratio=5.007; debt_capital=0.155; ret=-0.413; excess_ret=-0.448; vol=3120.000; shrout=3169.000; roe_chg1=-0.344; npm_chg1=missing; roa_chg1=-0.236; quick_ratio_lag1=6.555; de_ratio_lag1=0.186. top shap drivers: excess_ret increased model risk with shap=0.867 and value=-0.448; roe increased model risk with shap=0.731 and value=-1.465; quick_ratio reduced model risk with shap=-0.287 and value=3.454; npm increased model risk with shap=0.268 and value=missing; shrout increased model risk with shap=0.214 and value=3169.000.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Rank 2
permno: 14449
obs_date: 2020-01-31 00:00:00
predicted_pd: 0.1607
risk_decile: 10
